channel.title: 1 % Better - Hidden Stories in Sport
channel.description:
We often see the top layer or performance but its the lesser known stories, science and happenings where the real fascination and wonder lies. Join us as we discuss what is happening in sport, just that little bit deeper and differently to what you may expect.
Hosted by Xylon van Eyck & Dr David Lipman

item.title: Data vs Feel, Sex Tests & Short Cut Marathons
item.description:
After a few updates from things we discussed last week and a quick look at the LA marathon we discuss Strade Bianci, Ironman New Zealand and a half marathon world record at Lisbon half marathon. We then discuss some interesting topics for women in sport, including British athletes being required to pay for their own sex tests and Grayson Murphy’s new contract with Salomon. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:17) Medical Question

(04:42) LA Marathon 

(07:05) David’s update

(11:52) Community Shoutouts 

(19:12) USA Half Marathon Championships Update  

(23:11) LA Marathon Medals for 18 Miles 

(26:50) Strade Bianci

(33:58) Ironman New Zealand

(38:05) Half Marathon World Record 

(39:35) British Athletes Being Required to Pay for Their Own Sex Tests 

(41:47) Grayson Murphy Signs with Salomon 

(44:02) Impacts of Superspikes on Sprints 

(50:11) Doctor’s Office

(51:14) Seen on Social 

(56:26) What We’re Watching



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/1pb

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/1pb-planner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.


Article Discussed

https://sportrxiv.org/index.php/server/preprint/view/658/1429 

Articles David Mentioned He’d Published on CGM 

https://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/26/5/1624

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41773465/

https://healthperformancenexus.substack.com/p/earning-the-right

item.title: Training Surfaces & The Worst Coaching Job in the World
item.description:
After a big surprise, we discuss a listener question about training surfaces. Following this we touch on Louis Hincliffe’s decision to return to college and NIL implications, Keely Hodgkinson and Johannes Klaebo. We then discuss Tokyo marathon, the mess at the USA half marathon champs and the new European Marathon Classics. Finally we touch on a poll from the Athletic talking about the hardest job in coaching. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:42) Update

(01:41) Xylon’s Update

(14:00) David’s update

(20:12) Community Shoutouts 

(21:35) Listener Question - Training Surfaces

(25:37) Louis Hincliffe Heads back to College 

(28:00) Keely Hodgkinson

(32:08) Johannes Klaebo going to Cycling 

(35:48) Tokyo Marathon 

(41:14) USA Half Marathon Championships Mess 

(45:35) Molly Seidel’s Golden Ticket 

(48:11) European Marathon Classics 

(51:42) The Hardest Coaching Job in World Sport 

(59:29) Doctor’s Office

(1:08:00) Seen on Social 

(1:09:28) What We’re Watching

item.title: Boston Marathon and Balancing Crosstraining - Petter Engdahl Professional Trailrunner
item.description:
Petter Engdahl is a professional trailrunner and skier for the Team Adidas. Best known for his victory and course record at the CCC race at UTMB in 2022, Petter was 6th at the long trail world championships in 2025. Petter joins us on the back of the announcement he will be running the Boston Marathon. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:15) Xylon Update

(01:45) Dave Update 

(03:27) Interview Starts

(04:53) How Did Petter Get Into the Boston Field?

(06:05) What Motivated Petter to Run Boston?

(08:30) Tarawera 2026  

(11:07) Has Petter Ever Run a Road Race? 

(13:28) What Are Petter’s Aspirations for Boston? 

(18:14) What Boston Specific Training Will Petter Do? 

(23:55) How Much Running Does Petter do in a Week? 

(24:24) How Much Does Petter Run in Winter Normally? 

(27:10) Petter’s Coaching and Training Setup?

(29:40) Does Petter Normally Run on Roads? 

(32:31) Does Petter Do Any Crosstraining in Summer? 

(35:51) Crosscountry Skiing as a Crosstraining Tool 

(38:30) How Does Petter Think of Himself as an Athlete?

(42:10) Trailrunning Archetypes 

(45:07) What Has Petter Brought from Skiing to Trailrunning? 

(47:42) Morning Intervals for an Afternoon Race

(50:54) Petter’s Upcoming Race Plans

(54:41) Petter’s Love for Representing Sweden 

(59:34) Fueling Boston vs Trails



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.

item.title: Building a Performance Environment - Aitor Viribay
item.description:
In the first of a series we are calling ‘The Energy Series’ Aitor Viribay joins us to discuss how he is crafting a performance environment for Salomon’s professional trailrunning team. The lessons and insights have broad applicability including to the every day athlete! 

AItor Viribay is the Global sports performance director at Solomon, a consultant exercise physiologist at Girona football club, cofounder & chief scientific officer at Lab to field, cofounder & researcher at Methub and founder & director at Glut 4 science.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:53) Interview Starts

(05:23) Why Aitor was Brought to Salomon and his Remit

(07:25) How Did Aitor Approach Implementation of Cycling Concepts to Trailrunning

(11:21) Three Pillars of Impact for Trailrunning 

(19:18) How did Aitor Develop the Three Pillars of Need

(25:04) Talent Identification in Trailrunning

(28:55) Talent Development for Future Success

(35:24) What Does a Year for a Salomon Athlete Look Like?  

(43:52) Logistics as a Performance Advantage  

(53:45) Education as a Performance Strategy

(55:00) What has Aitor Brought Across to Trailrunning from Cycling 


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.


Links to Aitor:

Instagram: @aitorviribay 

X: @MVAitor 

Glut 4 Science Website: https://glut4science.com/

item.title: Developing Athletes in the UAE - Pete McKnight
item.description:
After some updates and a quick bit of what’s been happening we are straight into the interview with Pete McKnight. 

Pete McKnight is a performance consultant who advises a variety of professional teams and elite sporting organisations. In a career of over 25 years he has worked with organisations like Hiintsa Performance and Red Bull as well as having coached athletes for five Summer Olympics and two Winter Olympics. He is currently working in the UAE building pathways and systems to develop the next generation of Emirati athletes.  


(00:00) Introduction

(01:25) Xylon and Dave Updates 

(04:35) Femke Bol’s 800m debut

(06:00) Brigid Kosgei Changes Allegiances to Turkey 

(06:43) Anta Sport Buys Largest Stake in Puma 

(07:21) Grant Fisher to Run NYC Half Marathon 

(08:21) Athletes Banned for Betting on T&F 

(09:17) Interview Starts 

(10:44) Pete’s Remit in the UAE 

(14:14) Building Sports Performance Systems 

(17:18) Success Timelines for New System Development

(19:00) Role Modelling and Belief 

(22:09) What are the Highest Potential Sports for Short Term Success? 

(26:02) Talent Transfer 

(27:47) Talent Development System Design 

(32:15) Cultural Architecture  

(36:20) Hiring Professionals 

(37:20) What are the Biggest Challenges Pete has had to Navigate

(46:07) The Biggest Surprise Working in the UAE

(50:05) Surprising Cultural Nuances in the Culture of the UAE  

(50:53) How Did Pete’s Role in the UAE Come About? 

(56:50) How Did Pete Decide Taking the Role was a Good Idea? 

(1:00:13) Why Did Pete Step Out of Working as a Practitioner and How did He Develop the Skillset Outside of it 



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.


Links to Pete 

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/petejmcknight  

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/petemcknight/

item.title: Strength for Endurance, Planning a Training Week & Blood Flow Restriction
item.description:
Jonah Rosner is an Elite Running scientist and founder of Athletes Built in Science where he helps runners and hybrid athletes achieve their best performances.  He holds a bachelor’s degree from the University of Boston and a Masters in Exercise Physiology from the University of Texas in Austin. He is known for efforts in educating the running community where he posts science based content on instagram and in his newsletter which is read by almost 30 thousand runners. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(03:14) Interview Starts

(05:20) Jonah’s Running

(06:50) Jonah’s Running Coach and Why He Has a Coach

(10:35) Why Gaining Mass and Endurance at the Same Time is Hard 

(17:38) Planning a Training Week to Facilitate Strength Training and Endurance 

(22:51) Gaining vs Maintaining Adaptations

(27:50) Minimum Effective Dose of Strength Training in Runners

(34:14) What Has Jonah Learned from Force Plate Testing Runners?  

(36:58) Does Jonah Use Isometrics in Runners he Works with? 

(38:53) What is Blood Flow Restriction Training?

(43:08) Blood Flow Restriction Training for Recovery 

(47:28) Blood Flow Restriction Training for Warmup

(48:25) Blood Flow Restriction Training for Endurance Performance 

(56:51) Gait Kinematics and Biomechanical Load Using Stryd

item.title: Implications for Strava's IPO, Road Runners Going to Trails & Vice Versa and the Kipchoge Effect
item.description:
We have lots of happenings to catch up on and to help with this Dave is joined by a previous guest host Dan Squires. The guys chat about Strava’s IPO and its implications, NN Running’s rebrand, the Houston marathon and thoughts on race frequency. They then touch on some track news, Winter Olympics via Quinn Simmons’ aspirations there, review the Valencia 10k and discuss Molly Seidel’s trail debut. Finally they look at the Boston marathon fields and discuss some lesser hyped runners there. 


(00:00) Introduction

(01:30) Medical Question 

(04:00) Dan Update 

(07:30) Disillusionment with UTMB Organisation 

(09:35) Dave Update 

(14:53) Community Shoutouts 

(16:30) Reflecting on 100 Episodes and Podcast in General 

(20:30) Strava Prepares for IPO 

(27:28) Chat GPT and Claude Having Access to Apple Health & Health Connect

(28:32) NN Running Becomes DSM-Firmenich

(32:11) Houston Marathon 

(35:14) US Olympic Trials Thoughts 

(37:57) Gabby Thomas Running 400ms 

(40:45) Noah Lyles v Vernon Norwood 

(44:06) Quinn Simmons Wants to Medal in Summer & Winter Olympics 

(50:17) Valencia 10k 

(56:55) Molly Seidel Wins Bandera 50k and Sets CR 

(1:00:55) Des Linden to Marathon des Sables 

(1:02:12) Shelby Houlihan on the Trails 

(1:05:38) Boston Marathon Fields 

(1:13:43) Seen on Social/Dan’s Not a Fan 

(1:20:18) Doctor’s Office - Xylon 

(1:21:06) What We’re Watching

item.title: Coaches on the Hot Seat
item.description:
This week we discuss the absolute carnage that is the professional coaching realms at the moment. Coaches seem to be on the hot seat from the minute they’re hired and we seem to see a continuous merry-go-round of coaches and jobs. We are joined by Hein Kriek to discuss what has changed in the world and the factors that are driving the phenomenon. 

Hein Kriek is a Professional rugby coach, currently working as head coach of the Stellenbosch Academy of Sport Rugby International Institute and the head coach of Hamilton RFC.  

Hein’s coaching history includes; Director of Rugby Maritzburg College, Technical director of rugby Netherlands, assistant coach to Czech Republic rugby national team, Forwards Coach Mpumalanga Rugby Union (Pumas) and head coach of South African Schools. 


(00:00) Introduction

(04:38) Interview Starts 

(07:20) Are We Actually Seeing More Coaches Fired? 

(08:01) Where is the Impatience Coming From? 

(09:08) The Role of Private Equity 

(09:50) Societal Changes and The Changing of Fans 

(12:30) Seasons vs Quadrennal Cycles 

(13:30) Coaching in Different Environments 

(17:08) Developing Depth in a Squad 

(20:08) Changing Sporting Aspirations and Incentives  

(22:10) Losing the Dressing Room 

(29:15) The Role of the Coach and Organisational Alignment 

(34:04) How Much Impact Does a Coach Have? 

(39:23) Coaching Different Generations 

(44:06) Evolving as a Coach

(47:07) Focus Challenges in Athletes  

(49:02) Player Empowerment 

(54:50) Club vs Country Coaching 

(56:30) Disconnects Between Coaches and Players 

(59:30) Professional Coaches Getting Younger 

(1:03:25) Staying Current with What’s Happening 



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.

item.title: What Endurance Sports Can Learn from Team Sports - Dr Peter Tierney
item.description:
Dr Peter Tierney is a performance consultant who is currently consulting & advising a range of health & performance oriented individuals & companies. Peter has previously worked with Leinster rugby, the FA, Lululemon and Chelsea football club. He holds a BSc Health & Performance Science, MSc in Research and a PhD all completed from University College Dublin. In addition to this he holds a Professional Diploma in Data Analytics & Statistics. Peter joins us to discuss insights from tech and wearables as well as learnings for endurance athletes from team sports. 


(00:00) Introduction

(05:32) Interview Starts 

(07:27) Olympic Weightlifting 

(11:05) What Did Peter Learn from Team Sports Tech that He’s Bringing To Endurance Sports

(15:08) Perception vs Data 

(22:16) Recovery Points Systems 

(27:32) Resource Constraints and Individualisation

(30:49) Training Constraints 

(31:55) Weekly Organisation of Training

(38:35) Lessons for Endurance Sports Strength Training from Team Sports 

(47:40) Force Plate Testing for Endurance Athletes 

(1:02:36) 10-3-2-1 Sleep Formula 

(1:04:30) S.H.I.T Recovery Framework 

(1:07:43) What Wearables Does Peter Use and How Does he Use Them?

(1:13:45) Wearable Advice for Users

(1:19:37) What’s on the Horizon

item.title: AI in Sports - Professor Brinnae Bent
item.description:
In this episode we are joined by Professor Brinnae Bent to discuss AI in sport. 

Brinnae is a faculty member teaching AI and Cybersecurity at Duke University, director of the Duke TRUST Lab, and Executive Director of Tensor & Trust a trustworthy AI strategic consulting firm.

(00:00) Introduction

(00:17) Medical Question

(09:32) Interview Starts 

(11:40) What are Projects in the AI Realm in Sport that Excite Brinnae?

(30:17) Brinnae’s Disagree Bot

(36:25) Using AI as a Coach

(39:35) Can AI Help with Talent ID?  

(45:30) Digital Safety and Privacy 

(49:38) Who Should Own Your Wearable Data?

(1:01:21) Overtraining because of AI

(1:01:42) Discrepancies in AI Resources in Sport

Brinnae is a faculty member teaching AI and Cybersecurity at Duke University, director of the Duke TRUST Lab, and Executive Director of Tensor & Trust a trustworthy AI strategic consulting firm.

item.title: Running Culture & the Running Boom(s) - Mario Fraioli
item.description:
We are thrilled to be joined by Mario Fraioli on this episode. Marios is a running coach, author, podcaster and runner. Mario has coached at the highest levels, been a lifelong runner and has been writing and podcasting about running for decades. 

We discuss many parts of running with Mario, from the running booms to the state of US distance running to great races and books for listeners to check out. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:19) Medical Question

(07:06) Second Question

(08:22) Interview Starts

(11:06) The Third Running Boom 

(12:36) The First Running Boom 

(13:30) The Second Running Boom 

(15:00) Social Media and Media in the Running Boom 

(16:48) Gate Keeping in Running 

(19:12) Performance vs Participation 

(21:42) Is Mario Positive or Concerned About Where Running is? 

(23:52) Navigating Social Media Information in Running

(25:35) Mario’s Book 

(29:25) What Can Be Done to Improve Running Fan Numbers? 

(35:31) Races vs Time Trials

(37:55) Storytelling

(39:36) Sammy Wanjiru & His Impact on the Marathon 

(42:18) Great Races or Racers for People to Look Into 

(44:50) The State of US Distance Running?

(47:15) Does the NCAA System Concern Mario as a Development Pipeline?  

(49:14) How Does Mario Think Molly Seidel Will Go In Trailrunning? 

(52:19) Mario’s Thoughts on the State of Trailrunning 

(1:01:15) Mario’s Favourite Running Books  

(1:10:53) Review

item.title: HRV, HRV Guided Training and HRV Misconceptions - Dr Marco Altini
item.description:
In this episode we are joined by Dr Marco Altini, an entrepreneur, data scientist, endurance coach and runner. He is the founder and head of product at HRV4Training, a tool that is today trusted by more than 200 000 users, including olympic medalists, professional teams and the hosts of this podcast. Additionally Marco is a data science advisor to Oura and a Guest Lecturer at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam.   


(00:00) Introduction

(00:19) Medical Question

(05:44) Interview Starts 

(08:27) What is HRV 

(09:28) Can We Use HRV Through the Day to Measure Stress? 

(12:25) What Different Measures Can We Use to Measure HRV? 

(17:00) Pros and Cons of Morning vs Overnight HRV Measures 

(24:29) Athletes Using Wearables 

(29:19) How Does Marco Think About Competing with Wearable Industry Giants? 

(37:34) The Impact of Posture on HRV Measurement 

(43:03) What is ‘Good’ HRV? 

(48:03) Suppression of HRV 

(53:10) HRV Guided Training

(55:42) Seasonality in HRV Data

(58:03) Misconceptions Around HRV

(1:03:36) Does High HRV Mean You Should Train Harder? 

(1:06:47) Post Workout HRV

(1:11:25) Review

item.title: Western States, Blood Flow Restriction and Formula 1 Aid Stations - Pro Trailrunner Dan Jones
item.description:
In a huge episode, we discuss Courtney Dauwalter’s marathon efforts as well as Valencia marathon. Additionally we discuss rule changes and proposed rule changes in long jump and from FIFA. We also talk about a new sponsor for Hyrox and the implications of it as well as the IM 70.2 women’s WR being broken and why triathletes taking ‘gap years’ is helpful. 

We then get to our interview with Dan Jones. Dan Jones is a professional ultra trail runner who has 3 wins at Tarawera 100k, a top 5 at CCC race at UTMB and 3 top 5s at Western states, amongst a host of other impressive performances in trailrunning. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:19) Medical Question

(03:38) David & Xylon Updates

(09:10) Community Shoutouts 

(12:41) Courtney Dauwalter’s CIM Marathon

(14:51) IM 70.3 Women’s WR

(19:32) FIFA New Rule Trial 

(25:18) Valencia Marathon 

(33:46) Interview Starts 

(36:30) Dan’s ‘Multisport’ Background

(39:00) Does Dan Have Any Thoughts of Returning to ‘Multisport’ Racing? 

(40:00) Cross Training 

(42:22) What Does a Training Week for Dan Look Like? 

(44:45) How Does Training Change Across a Year? 

(48:07) Who’s in Dan’s Support Team

(50:05) Why Western States?

(51:55) What Are Areas for Improvement for Dan at Western States? 

(53:46) Improvement in Heat Training

(55:14) How is Dan Thinking About Racing in the Second Half of the Year? 

(57:58) How Does Dan Monitor Training Adaptation and Recovery? 

(1:01:27) Training in Kenya  

(1:04:14) Does Dan Have Any Road Marathon Desires? 

(1:05:04) Is Dan Interested in Road Ultramarathons? 

(1:06:30) Do PTL or Tor des Geants Interest Dan? 

(1:07:27) Aid Stations at Western States and Gut Training  

(1:11:08) Intensity of Running at Western States  

(1:12:50) Pack Dynamics and Pacing 

(1:16:08) Balancing Family on Race Day 

(1:17:43) Review

(1:18:36) Doctor’s Office - Pickel ball 

(1:21:16) What We’re Watching

We then get to our interview with Dan Jones. Dan Jones is a professional ultra trail runner who has 3 wins at Tarawera 100k, a top 5 at CCC race at UTMB and 3 top 5s at Western states, amongst a host of other impressive performances in trailrunning.

item.title: Supershoe Use, Being Self-Coached & Heat Training - Pro Runner Alfie Manthorpe
item.description:
We have a few announcements, including a competition this week. Following this we follow up on the Premier League salary cap measures which have been put in place and then discuss a paper on adoption of wearables by coaches. We then move to our interview with Alfie Manthorpe, a professional distance runner who was the top British finisher at the Valencia half marathon.

item.title: Is an Energy Deficit Really That Bad? - Associate Professor Jose Areta
item.description:
After recapping some of Xylon’s life choices, we discuss some outrageous Lebron James stats before a quick cricket discussion. We then move on to some discussion of refereeing and following this discuss a paper on the evolution of endurance performance and coaching. Finally Dr Jose Areta joins us to discuss a paper of his. 

Jose Areta is an Associate Professor in Exercise metabolism and nutrition at Liverpool John Moores University currently specializing on the endocrine, metabolic and physiological effects of low energy availability and how these may affect performance. Jose kindly joined us to discuss his recent publication: “Endocrine, Metabolic, and Skeletal Muscle Proteomic Responses During Energy Deficit With Concomitant Aerobic Exercise in Humans”


(00:00) Introduction

(00:17) Medical Question

(02:37) Xylon’s Bad Life Choices 

(05:47) David’s Update 

(08:49) Community Shoutouts 

(13:14) Lebron James Longevity

(15:17) The Ashes 

(18:07) Referees in Rugby 

(25:12) Evolution of Endurance Coaching and Performance 

(30:27) Interview Preview 

(31:33) Interview Starts 

(33:10) Context for the Paper

(35:48) Idea Behind the Paper 

(37:53) What was Done in the Paper 

(41:08) What Does a Study Like This Cost? 

(42:07) How Long did the Study Take? 

(44:07) How Much Would Including Women Have Cost? 

(46:02) What Did the Authors Find? 

(50:41) What was the Fat Free Mass Loss Comprised of?

(56:01) Muscles and Mitochondria vs Collagen

(1:00:27) Would a Similar Pattern be Expected with a More Moderate but Longer Term Energy Deficit?

(1:02:43) What is the Next Step in the Research?

(1:07:29) Energy Deficit and Performance 

(1:09:32) Would Results be Different with Different Exercise Modalities?

(1:14:17) Review 

(1:18:52) Doctor’s Office - Hannah Williams

(1:22:05) What We’re Watching

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.

item.title: Brain Health, Concussion and CTE
item.description:
We have a bumper episode for you this week, starting with people running - Jim Walmsley in a road 10k and pro cyclists in their offseason. We touch on some previous topics which are back in the news; salary caps for cycling and Imogen Simmonds’ race plans. We then discuss hydration testing to prevent weight cutting in E-sports, the mes at T100 Dubai and Valencia Marathon fields. We finish with an interview around concussion and brain health in general with Associate Professor Tommy Wood.  

Associate Professor Tommy Wood is Neuroscientist, Researcher, and Athletic Performance Consultant. He currently works in the department Pediatrics and Neuroscience at the University of Washington where his laboratory focuses on brain health across the lifespan – conducting research that focuses on ways to treat a range of brain injuries.


(00:00) Introduction

(00:57) Medical Question

(05:15) Xylon’s update

(06:07) David’s Update 

(09:37) Community Shoutouts 

(13:44) Listener Question - Heat Training for Cold Performance 

(17:30) Jim Walmsley Runs a Road 10k

(18:24) UCI Proposes Salary Cap 

(19:45) Imogen Simmonds Returns to Racing 

(20:23) Cyclists Running 

(23:12) Women’s 100 mile World Record Broken

(24:12) UCI Does HydrationTests Before E-Sports World Championships 

(26:20) T100 Doha Mess 

(32:30) Valencia Marathon Elite Fields  

(37:22) Interview Starts 

(39:50) Are Concussions Related to Brain Diseases? 

(44:31) Will We See More Cases of Brain Disease in the Future? 

(48:39) How Does Tommy Think About Capacity of the Brain?  

(54:32) Core Buckets of Brain Health and Healthy Brain Aging 

(1:07:18) Strength vs Endurance Training for Brain Health

(1:14:55) Are We Nearing a Diagnostic Concussion Test 

(1:20:26) Review

(1:21:04) Seen on Social  

(1:23:14) Doctors Office

item.title: Earnings, 120g/hr of Carbs & Transitioning Sports
item.description:
In a wide ranging episode, we discuss people transitioning sports, review the Ironman 70.3 world championships and examine some research on carbohydrate intake in elite marathoners. We also discuss salary caps and their implications for global leagues as a few leagues evaluate them. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:18) Medical Question

(05:06) David’s Update

(06:51) Xylon’s update

(19:58) Louis Rees-Zammit Transitions back to Rugby 

(24:29) Springboks vs France 

(27:08) Kipchoge and NYC Race Earnings 

(32:30) Marbella 70.3 World Championships 

(40:40) Salary Caps

item.title: Move Aside Ozempic, Apitegromab is Here (Soon)
item.description:
Xylon and David give their updates before discussing the Women’s Cricket World Cup, Kipchoge’s plans going forward (and that shirt), they review NYC marathon and discuss the potential for weightloss drugs to be banned (including one on the horizon). They also discuss some interesting research on cooling and marathon durability.

(38:44) Kipchoge’s Plans 

(41:00) Kipchoge’s Cooling Top 

(42:46) Weightloss Drugs Being Banned for the LA Olympics  

(49:00) Aligning The World Rugby Calendar

(54:44) Durability in Marathons

(1:00:57) New York Marathon

(1:08:27) Increasing Numbers of Women Running sub 3

item.title: Advice for Heat Training with Dr Lindsey Hunt
item.description:
In a huge episode we cover a bunch of news, including changes to the NYC marathon fields, Molly Seidel’s running plans, Valencia half marathon results and Capetown Marathon happenings. We then discuss the Imogen Simmonds case verdict, some Rugby World Cup Qualification drama and Big’s Backyard Ultra World record. We then get into the more significant discussions of the episode; the Treadmill World Championships aka “Run X” and World Triathlon approving fitness racing and Swim Run as triathlon disciplines. 

Finally we interview Dr Lindsey Hunt around heat training. Lindsey is a senior sports scientist at Precision Fuel and Hydration specialising in endurance exercise performance optimisation with a focus on extreme environments. He currently oversees the Precision Performance Lab, which delivers physiological testing, heat training, and personalised fueling and hydration strategies for athletes of all levels. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:17) Medical Question

(02:52) Xylon’s update

(05:00) David’s Update 

(07:56) Community Shoutouts 

(10:08) Listener Comments on Ketones

(19:35) NYC Marathon Field Changes 

(21:30) Capetown Marathon 

(27:31) Imogen Simmonds Cleared of Wrongdoing 

(31:14) Molly Seidel Goes to Trailrunning

(32:11) Big’s Backyard Ultra 

(32:44) RWC Qualification Controversy

(33:37) Treadmill World Championships - “Run X”

(37:38) World Triathlon Approves Fitness Racing and Swim Run as Disciplines 

(39:36) Interview Starts  

(42:08) Does Heat Training Help in the Cold 

(43:21) Is There a Lower Bound of Cold Where Heat Training Would Stop Helping? 

(44:18) Is Body Size a Consideration for the Benefits of Heat Training? 

(47:06) Risks of Heat Training

(48:49) How Should We Dose Heat Training? 

(51:53) Intensity and Duration of Heat Training

(54:34) Caffeine and Heat 

(1:01:36) Supplements and Heat Tolerance

(1:04:17) Sweating and Sweat Rate in Heat Training 

(1:08:11) Common Mistakes with Heat Training

(1:12:25) Advice for Heat Training

(1:15:54) Review 

(1:17:15) Doctor’s Office

(1:19:55) Seen on Social 

(1:21:48) What We’re Watching

(1:22:33) Sports Equinox

item.title: Cyclists Trying to Run & Kenyan Scholarships
item.description:
In this episode we review some world records, look at another scathing doping survey and check in on Amsterdam marathon results - including looking at why cyclists struggle to convert to runners. We then chat about 2026 plans for both Athlos and the T100 as well as looking at an interesting article about US colleges recruiting Kenyan runners. We also chat to Eloise du Luart about her experiences with heat training and look at the UCI press release about ketone use.  


(00:00) Introduction

(00:17) Medical Question

(04:00) David’s Update 

(04:53) Xylon’s update

(08:00) Community Shoutouts 

(12:50) Heat Training with Pro Athlete Eloise du Luart 

(20:49) Remi Bonnet Break VK WR 

(23:55) 24hr Women’s WR Broken 

(26:16) Commonwealth Games Athletes Doping

(28:10) Amsterdam Marathon Results 

(30:44) Tom Dumoulin in Amsterdam  

(34:17) Geraint Thomas Starts Running

(35:33) Americans Added to the Valencia Marathon Field 

(38:25) Differences in Taper for Elites and Amateurs 

(40:04) UCI Press Release on Ketones

(47:32) Where’s the Line for Doping?

(49:45) Athlos’ Plans for 2026 

(51:53) T100 Changes for 2026

(57:46) Colleges Scouting Kenyan Runners

item.title: Amsterdam Marathon Preparation - Pro Marathoner Phil Sesemann
item.dc:creator: 1% Better Podcast

item.title: New Records, New Olympic Events & Lawsuits
item.description:
We start off with a discussion of the performance cycle of sports teams before discussing a world class performance to break the SA 10k record. We touch on some Hyrox, Kilian Jornet’s States of Elevation and the potential for cross country to join the winter Olympics. In other Olympic news we discuss some events in the T&F being held outside of the stadium before more in-depth discussions of the Strava vs Garmin situation and a Kona preview with Pro Triathlete Eloise du Luart. We then debut a new segment; ‘seen on social’ before finishing with the Doctor’s office and a brief chat about  Connor Mantz’s chances of breaking the American marathon record. 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:00) Medical Question

(05:00) David’s Update 

(07:24) Xylon’s Update

(12:35) Community Shoutouts 

(16:20) Cycles and Patterns in Team Performance  

(23:33) SA 10K Record Broken

(26:11) Hyrox World Record Broken

(31:53) Kilian Jornet States of Elevation

(24:45) Cross-Country Joining the Winter Olympics 

(36:37) LA Olympics Having T&F Events Outside the Stadium

(38:07) Strava Sues Garmin

(43:23) Kona Preview with Pro Triathlete Eloise Du Luart 

(59:04) SOS - HR PRs 

(1:01:15) Doctors Office Phill Bowden

(1:05:14) What We’re Watching

item.title: Berlin Marathon Drama, Frodeno Goes Long & Supershoe Science
item.description:
We are back and covering off the latest happenings in the sporting world. We cover Jan Frodeno doing a 55k trail race, a hot day at the Berlin Marathon and a quick review of the world championships cycling in Kigali, Rwanda. Then we touch on the Boston Marathon cutoff time before some scientific papers. The papers look at traditional fueling vs CGM informed fueling and whether the benefits of supershoes persist after hours. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:21) Medical Question

(03:58) Xylon’s Update from Time in Rwanda

(09:30) David’s Update 

(15:00) Community Shoutouts 

(19:00) Listener Question on Altitude 

(22:45) Jan Frodeno Does Nice by UTMB Race

(24:26) Berlin Marathon Results 

(27:44) Cycling World Championships in Rwanda

(35:41) Boston Marathon Entry Cutoff 

(37:51) Traditional vs CGM Based Fueling

(44:39) Do Supershoes Still Work as Well After Hours

(51:55) Doctors Office Josh Kerr

(53:25) Doctors Office Tyreek Hill 

(57:55) What We’re Watching

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: Fasting, Heat and Do Endurance Athletes Have more Daughters?
item.description:
We have a special guest host this week, Kristi Storoschuk joins the podcast again to help David while Xylon is away. After a listener question on fueling they jump into some research. They discuss some fascinating papers including the impact of heat on carbohydrate and glycogen utilisation rates, the effects of a 7 day fast on performance and a paper looking at whether endurance athletes have more daughters. They then step into Kristi’s world and look at a paper on mitochondria and why they are more than just the powerhouses of the cell! 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:44) Medical Question

(01:40) Kristi’s Madeira Trip

(02:38) Kristi’s PhD Update

(03:29) Kristi’s Training Update 

(06:01) Community Shoutouts Listener Question on Fueling

(13:21) Heat and Dehydration’s Impact on Carbohydrate & Glycogen Use 

(24:10) Effect of Sport on Sex of Offspring

(32:04) Performance Impacts of 7 Days of Fasting

(45:04) Mitochondria



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Thanks also to Stryd for their sponsorship, check out Stryd:

Stryd helps you optimize your training and racing by measuring your running power, providing real-time data to improve efficiency, pace, and performance on any terrain. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.


Review on Carbohydrate & Glycogen Use in Heat 

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-025-02294-3 

Article on Sports and Sex of Children

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-05445-8 

Article on 7 Days of Fasting Performance

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-55418-0

item.title: Engineering a UTMB Win
item.description:
We start this week off with some NYC Marathon news before diving into some recent doping happenings. After this we review the Ironman World Championships in Nice before a look at the World Track and Field Championships. Finally we have our interview with Joseph Mestrallet. 

Joseph Mestrallet is a data scientist ,CEO and Founder of Enduraw. Additionally he is currently the team manager at Nutri-pure, where he works with multiple elite athletes with a deep conviction: mathematics are the keys to reach the last few percent of performance. Joseph is the data scientist who helped engineer wins at UTMB for Tom Evans and Ruth Croft. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:25) Medical Question

(03:40) David’s Update and Time in Nice 

(08:00) Community Shoutouts 

(12:53) NYC Marathon Announces Sifan Hassan and Eliud Kipchoge 

(16:33) Joyline Chegnego Positive Test 

(19:15) Raven Saunders Whereabouts Failures 

(20:40) Diribe Welteji Test Refusal and Ban Upheld by CAS 

(21:58) Sebastian Sawe Extra Testing Pre Berlin 

(23:49) Ironman World Championships Nice 

(29:05) Track & Field World Championships 

(30:15) Predictions Update 

(32:34) Falls in the Races 

(32:24) False Starts and the Starter

(35:24) Discuss Body Types  

(37:38) Field Event GOATs

(39:23) Why is the USA so Successful 

(41:21) World Championships Broadcast 

(42:18) Narrow Margins of Victory 

(43:28) Observations from the Marathons 

(49:09) Other Observations from the World Championships

(52:30) Interview Starts 

(55:55) What Other Projects has Joseph Worked on? 

(59:24) What Data Does Joseph Collect for his Planning? 

(1:01:32) What Goes Into Wholistic Performance? 

(1:08:09) How Close was Joseph’s Pacing Plan to Actual Performance? 

(1:10:29) What Sort of Testing did Joseph do to Prepare the Pacing Plans? 

(1:14:00) Predictions Margins of Error 

(1:16:17) What Live Data was Joseph Giving During the Race?

(1:19:27) Is This Data Informed Preparation Against the Spirit of Trailrunning? 

(1:20:17) What Live Data was Joseph Giving During the Race?

(1:23:24) How is Joseph Thinking about AI in his Work? 

(1:30:00) Doctors Office

(1:31:06) What We’re Watching

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.

item.title: Shoe controversy, Boston marathon training & Thyroid hormone use in Olympians
item.description:
We start off with a listener question about recovery runs before diving into the Hayden Wilde shoe saga and a mess at the Tokyo World Championships track & field with the warm up track. We then discuss whether the women’s & men’s races being on the same day for Paris Roubaix is going to be good for the women or not before briefly previewing the Ironman World Championships. We finish with two research papers, one on use (or overuse) of thyroid hormones in Olympians and one on the training characteristics of Boston Marathon runners. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:50) Medical Question

(03:40) David’s Update 

(05:20) Xylon’s Update

item.dc:creator: 1% Better Podcast
item.itunes:summary:
We start off with a listener question about recovery runs before diving into the Hayden Wilde shoe saga and a mess at the Tokyo World Championships track & field with the warm up track. We then discuss whether the women’s & men’s races being on the same day for Paris Roubaix is going to be good for the women or not before briefly previewing the Ironman World Championships. We finish with two research papers, one on use (or overuse) of thyroid hormones in Olympians and one on the training characteristics of Boston Marathon runners.

item.title: Recovery From an Eating Disorder Pro Cyclist Clara Koppenburg
item.description:
Before our interview, we cover a listener question and then take a look back at some Diamond League and forward to UTMB. We also discuss teenage phenoms Cooper Luteknhaus and Gout Gout. Finally we hear from Ashleigh Moolman-Pasio and Hannah Walker regarding weight in the Women’s peloton. 

Clara Koppenburg is a professional road cyclist known for her versatility and climbing strength. She was encouraged to start cycling by her father who is a team doctor. Armed with a sports science degree (University of Konstanz, completed in 2018), Clara blends intellect, athleticism, and commitment to her craft. 

What truly sets Clara apart is her courageous openness about her personal challenges. In a recent, deeply personal revelation, she shared her journey through an eating disorder, the loss of her menstrual cycle for six years, and her path to recovery. She declared, “I won myself back”, emphasizing that reclaiming her health was her greatest victory—even without big race wins.

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:22) Medical Question

(02:00) David’s Running  

(04:44) David’s UTMB Experience

(19:49) Gout Gout Runs Sub 10 Seconds 

(21:29) Fastest Ever 100k Run

(25:26) Diamond League Lausanne 

(28:30) Diamond League Brussels 

(39:48) David’s Tokyo World Championships Predictions 

(36:52) UTMB Observations 

(43:07) Weight in Women’s Cycling 

(44:15) Ashleigh Moolman-Pasio’s Thoughts 

(52:19) Hannah Walker’s Thoughts  

(59:14) Interview Starts

(1:01:11) Did Clara Expect the Response from her Instagram Post 

(1:04:04) How Is Clara’s Experience Day to Day Now vs When Suffering?  

(1:12:28) What Made Clara Make a Change?   

(1:17:00) How Does Clara Navigate the Performance Environment? 

(1:24:25) What Can Teams Do Better To Support Riders? 

(1:29:39) Should Cycling Have Screening Pre Race to Prevent RED-S?

(1:36:12) Clara’s Lack of Body Dysmorphia 

(1:39:50) Clara’s Cookbook 

(1:42:50) Review 

(1:43:54) What We Will Be Watching

item.title: Marathon Longevity with Multiple Olympian Lisa Weightman
item.description:
Before our guest joins us, Xylon learns to tie shoelaces, we cover a listener question on heart rate during warm up and we review the Diamond League in Silesia. We then touch on the records from the Leadville 100 before a look at the Chicago and Sydney Marathon fields. 

Our guest, joining us to discuss her upcoming Sydney Marathon is Lisa Weightman is a 4 time olympic marathon representative, qualifying for her first olympics in her debut marathon in London 2008 running 2:32:32. Her marathon personal best is 2:23:15 from Osaka 2023, and she has run a 68:48 half marathon. Beyond her Olympic achievements, she won a Silver medal at the 2018 Delhi commonwealth games and a bronze in the 2010 New Delhi commonwealth games. She’s a a 3 time top 8 finisher at world marathon majors, twice national champion in the half marathon and the winner of the 2013 Melbourne marathon, 2010 Nagano olympic commemorative marathon and 2009 Christchurch half marathon. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:16) Medical Question

(05:15) Xylon’s Update

item.title: Should You Do More Zone 2 Training?
item.description:
We have a huge episode this week. Most importantly we discuss yoghurt gate but we also touch on a bunch of community shoutouts, some NFL, Crossfit, T100, UK & US track & field and the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift all before we get to our interview. 

Our guest is Kristi Storoschuk who is a PhD Candidate in Muscle Physiology at Queen’s University with the goal of contributing to our understanding of diet, food timing, exercise, and metabolism. She is passionate and curious about all things diet, lifestyle, metabolic flexibility, aging, and longevity. She is the lead author on a recent paper 

 

(00:00) Introduction

item.title: Microdosing, Elite Athlete's healing capacity and Jan Frodeno Running OCC
item.description:
This week starts with some absolute abhorrent behviour from Xylon before we get into some great community shoutouts. We then touch on athletes and accelerated recovery rates, why the NFL is hard to transition to for rugby players and Jan Frodeno’s chances at the OCC race at UTMB. We discuss the beer mile world record, and then our big topics; injuries and illness in cycling (how common are they?) and some research on microdosing testosterone and EPO. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:20) Medical Question

(02:00) Xylon’s Abomination

(06:30) Xylon’s Update

(09:10) David’s Update

(12:21) Community Shoutouts

(19:38) Hayden Wilde Doing T100 London 

(22:06) UTMB Fields & Jan Frodeno

(28:16) Louis Rees-Zammit returns to Rugby 

(29:43) Beer Mile World Record Broken

(31:36) Grand Slam Track Yet to Pay Athletes

(34:10) Tour de France Review 

(46:48) Microdosing EPO and Testosterone to Beat Tests

(51:23) Ruth Chepngetich 

(53:40) Injury and Illness in Cycling Disciplines

(57:56) Doctor’s Office - Cycling Edition 

(1:03:27) What We’re Watching

item.title: Mental Health vs Mental Resilience
item.description:
Gary Fahey is a mental strength and performance specialist, mental health and addiction counsellor, public speaker and bestselling author. He currently works across the corporate and sporting spaces helping high-performing professionals and teams overcome crises, build resilience, and achieve sustainable success through his unique blend of experience and qualifications. 

Gary’s story is a fascinating one and has shaped who he is and how he works. He holds multiple degrees and is currently undertaking a PhD in Psychology & Conscious Decision Making. Earlier in his career Gary spent 18 years with the Australian Federal Police, including personal protection for prime minister Kevin Rudd. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:10) Xylon Bullies David

(04:29) Community Shoutouts

(05:29) Listener Correction - IM Qualification System

(10:24) Community Shoutouts Continued

(17:46) Interview Starts

(21:14) Gary’s Background 

(22:38) Externally Bulletproof, Internally Brittle 

(24:55) Fallout from Misuse of Company Credit Card 

(30:01) How Were Things When News Broke? 

(32:13) How Did Gary Decide to Move Into Neuroscience? 

(34:55) Has the World or Have Athletes Changed? 

(39:33) Will Improving Mental Health Make Athletes Lose Their Edge? 

(42:15) How Do You Balance Short & Long Term Goals & Performance? 

(45:22) How Does Gary Start a Conversation with High Performers? 

(48:13) Mental Health Struggle vs Mental Resilience? 

(52:00) How Do You Help Someone Who’s Achieved a Massive Career Goal? 

(55:00) Professional Athletes and Their Identity? 

(57:24) How Do You Start Working on Identity Outside of Sport? 

(1:01:45) How do People Overcome Slipping Off Performance Habits? 

(1:07:33) Advice from Gary for Universal Performance

(1:14:41) Sports Psychology is More than “Flow” 

(1:18:00) Optimisation vs Perfection

(1:20:34) The Most Difficult Situation Gary Had in His Time Protecting The PM

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

Plus, it integrates seamlessly with your favorite running watch for real time guidance! Go to https://stryd.live/opb to export Stryd’s cutting-edge technology and how it can empower your run.

item.title: Diet, Stress Balance and Course Familiarisation
item.description:
We tackle a number of listener questions in the episode covering topics like stress balance and coping, recovery nutrition, diet, microbiome and nutrition for sleep. We also discuss the value of training on the course you will race on and why this may be helpful.

(32:20) Fibre in Athletic Performance

(39:51) Recovery Nutrition from Events

(57:28) Specific vs General Conditioning

item.title: Small Margins & Marginal Gains with Dr Sam Shepherd
item.description:
Prior to our interview which is all about crewing and marginal gains we discuss Tracksmith’s new marathon team, why Nerd Clusters are gaining popularity for fueling and athletes changing countries. 

Our guest is Dr Sam Shepherd is the head of sports science at Precision Fuel & Hydration and co-director and sports nutritionist at total endurance nutrition. Sam joins us to discuss his experience and learnings from crewing a top 5 finisher at Western States 100. We discuss the narrow margins and marginal gains from crewing including nuances of Western States specifically. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:20) Medical Question

(08:14) Xylon’s Update

(11:17) David’s Update

(15:17) Community Shoutouts

(17:15) Tracksmith Launches a Women’s Marathon Team  

(19:07) Grand Slam Track’s Payment Issues

(22:09) Nerd Clusters for Endurance Fuel 

(25:33) T&F Athletes Changing Countries 

(34:29) Other Sport Examples 

(38:37) What is it Like Representing Another Country

(43:25) Interview Starts

(45:25) What was Sam’s Role at Western States

(49:41) How Do you Build a Fueling Strategy? 

(52:07) How Does the Environment Modify Things? 

(55:18) Heat as a Fueling Challenge?

(56:50) Why Does Precision Have So Many Athletes Performing Well at Western States?

(58:40) What’s Driven the Increase in Pack Usage at Western States?

(1:01:45) Planning Aid Stations

(1:04:21) Pre Race Preparations for Aid Stations 

(1:06:45) How Much of the Fueling Plan is Planned vs Adapted?  

(1:09:27) What Are Some Common Mistakes in Crewing? 

(1:10:35) What Stuck out to Sam at Western States?  

(1:12:41) Can You Cool Too Much at Western States? 

(1:14:46) Review 

(1:16:24) Doctor’s Office - Noah Lolesio 

(1:17:37) What We’re Watching

item.title: The Wildest Week in Endurance Sport
item.description:
It’s been a huge week in endurance sport and specifically for Hiroki Kai who we talk about. We also discuss changes to qualification for the Ironman world championships, Challenge Roth result and private jet sponsorship controversy. We then recap the Eugene Diamond League with some insights and observations. Of course all our regular segments are here too.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:19) Medical Question

(06:52) David’s Update

(10:19) Xylon’s Update

(14:09) Treadmill Struggles vs Altitude 

(16:30) Community Shoutouts

(21:45) Changes to Qualification for IM World Championships 

(26:55) Challenge Roth

item.title: Inside Breaking 4
item.description:
A big weekend in endurance sport sees us recap Western States, Ironman Frankfurt, the breaking of the backyard ultra world record and a quick discussion into East Africans in Trailrunning. We also touch on Sydney Marathon Elite fields and Challenge Roth drug testing age groupers.  

Our guest is Robby Ketchell, a sports scientist and aerodynamics expert who helped engineer the Ineos 1:59 project with Eliud Kipchoge and more recently worked on the Sub 4 Project with Faith Kipyegon. He joins us to discuss the project. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:30) Medical Question

(03:53) David’s Update

(04:56) Xylon’s Update

(11:58) Community Shoutouts

(13:23) Correction

(15:03) Toulouse Win Top 14 

(15:40) Backyard Ultra World Record Broken

(19:00) Tyreek Hill Runs 10.10

(20:22) IM Frankfurt 

(23:32) Western States 

(28:30) Marathon du Mont Blanc

(30:10) Will East Africans Start Dominating Trailrunning? 

(32:20) Challenge Roth Drug Testing Age Groupers

(34:55) Sydney Marathon Elite Field

(37:01) Interview Starts 

(39:01) How Were Things on the Ground? 

(41:40) Did Robby Think Sub 4 was Possible? 

(44:03) What Tradeoffs Needed to be Made? 

(46:29) Where Else Could Things be Improved with Hindsight?  

(49:42) Explaining the Pacing Formation

(55:13) Would Faith Have Been Better off Running Further Out?

(58:20) How Fast Could Faith Run in an Optimised Road Mile?

(1:01:25) What’s Next for Robby?

(1:03:30) Doctor’s Office - Vale Bill Dellinger 

(1:05:11) What We’re Watching

item.title: Pro Runner Colleen Quigley - The Future of Running as a Team Sport
item.description:
After some quick updates and community shoutouts we hear from previous guest Matt Marquardt about his IM Cairns win. Before discussing the Paris Diamond League, Tyreek Hill vs Noah Lyles and the value of running a hard mile for long distance runners. 

Our guest is Colleen Quigley is a professional middle distance runner representing the USA in the Rio 2016 Olympics. She joins us to discuss Meridia, a new women-led, athlete-owned track team, based in Boulder. It’s focused on high-performance, strategic storytelling, and shoe brand-agnostic sponsorship opportunities for its athletes. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:18) Medical Question

(04:54) Xylon’s Update

(07:20) David’s Update

(10:42) Corrections

(11:58) Accuracy and Precision in Distance Measured in Running 

(14:50) Community Shoutouts

(18:30) Update from Matt Marquardt 

(28:11) Paris Diamond League 

(44:17) Noah Lyles vs Tyreek Hill

(47:21) Value in Running a Mile

(50:13) Interview Starts

(51:40) What is the Origin of the name “Meridia”?  

(53:08) Why Now?

(57:20) How was the Coaching Advisor Chosen? 

(1:00:02) The Performance Environment

(1:05:02) How Do Pro Runners Make a Living? 

(1:06:08) What is Collen’s Approach to Storytelling? 

(1:10:10) Meridia Mile

(1:17:10) How Were the Founding Members Chosen?  

(1:23:41) Adding Members to the Team

(1:26:58) Review 

(1:29:08) What We’re Watching

item.title: Determinants of Success in Tennis
item.description:
Aside from our normal segments we quickly discuss Grand Slam Track cancelling their LA slam, some friction issues at the Hyrox world champs and the Dauphine. We touch on the new Boston Marathon qualifying changes, Diamond League Stockholm and then the new tech Visma Lease a BIke are Using. We then get into the interview about determinants of tennis success following on from Roland Garros. 

Our guest is Hugh Clarke, a professional tennis coach and analyst living in Canada. He holds a Masters of Sport psychology from McGill University, writes one of the most widely read tennis analysis newsletters and consults to professional tennis players and their coaches.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:30) Medical Fact Checking

(04:37) David’s Update

(06:33) Xylon’s Update

(08:46) Why Would You Use a Running Power Meter? 

(13:01) Community Shoutouts

(16:53) Grand Slam Track Cancels LA Slam 

(18:37) Friction at Hyrox World Championships

(20:31) Dauphine Results 

(23:22) Boston Marathon Qualification Changes 

(27:05) Diamond League Stockholm  

(31:14) Visma Lease a Bike Using Respiratory Sensors

(37:38) Interview Starts

(40:20) What Determines Success in Tennis 

(42:25) What are Minimum Requirements for Elite Tennis? 

(45:05) What has Contributed to the Change in Tennis Tactics? 

(49:33) Racquet Technology 

(51:49) Controversy About Tennis Balls

(52:40) Psychology of Tennis and Momentum 

(59:55) Nutrition & Fueling in Tennis 

(64:30) Travel and Logistics Challenges on Tour 

(65:10) Training Around Tournaments 

(69:12) Differences in Mens and Womens Tennis 

(71:30) What to Watch for in Tennis 

(75:00) Review 

(75:58) Doctor’s Office - Michael Matthews 

(75:53) What We’re Watching 

(79:53) Xylon Gloats

item.title: Fast Times and How Sport Evolves
item.description:
In a huge episode, we cover a large range of things this week, mostly around fast performances and how sport evolves. We announce our new sponsor, update listeners on ourselves and the evolution of some things we covered previous and have our community shoutouts. We then discuss fast Ironman performances, Killian Jornet, Diamond League, Sydney McLaughlan-Levrone and the Giro d’Italia. We also discuss an observation on how sports like gravel cycling evolve.


(00:00) Introduction

(00:30) Doctor’s Question

(03:41) David’s Update 

(05:57) Xylon’s Update

(08:40) Announcement 

(11:45) Community Shoutouts

(16:52) David’s Sporting History 

(20:19) Listener Message - Ideal Diet for Athletes

(25:03) IM Hamburg 

(30:40) Fastest IM Marathon

(33:32) Sydney McLaughlan-Levrone’s Talent

(37:58) World Aquatics Bans Everyone Associated with Enhanced Games

(42:52) Killian Jornet Seeks a Pacer

(45:08) Diamond League Rome

(47:41) How Sports Evolve 

(53:04) Giro d’Italia Insights

(58:21) House vs NCAA

(69:18) Doctor’s Office

(74:51) What We’re Watching

item.title: Pro Ultra Trailrunner Emily Hawgood - Canyons 100 win & Western States Prep
item.description:
Emily Hawgood is a Professional trail runner for team Adidas Terrex. Hot off the heels of a high profile win at the 100k Canyons endurance run by UTMB she’s been top 10 at western states & UTMB for the last 4 years, though in 2023 this was in the CCC race at the UTMB festival. She has also previously won ultra trail Cape town.

In this chat we discuss Canyons, prep for UTMB & Western states, fueling, training, cross training and all the other good things.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:25) Medical Question

(06:20) David’s Update

(06:40) Xylon’s Update

(13:31) Interview Starts

(15:47) How did Emily End Up at The College of Idaho? 

(18:01) How Does Emily Think About Family & Community Living Away from Family?

(20:55) Emily’s Transition into Trailrunning

(24:29) Canyons 100 Recap 

(28:14) What is a Golden Ticket?

(31:22) How Does Canyons Fit into the Western States Build? 

(34:55) Decreasing Margins of Victory in Trailrunning

(37:55) The Professionalisation of Trailrunning 

(38:41) How does Emily Deal with Pressure at Western States?

(41:11) What Does Prep Look Like for Western States vs Canyons?

(41:42) Recovery from an Ultramarathon

(47:10) Offseason and Returning to Training

(51:50) Are There Differences in Preparation for Western States Year to Year? 

(55:40) Longevity in Running 

(58:22) Being a Low Mileage Athlete 

(60:12) How Does Emily Perform on Such Low Mileage? 

(64:33) Eating in Ultra Endurance 

(66:03) Fueling Pre and In Race

(72:45) Differences in Fueling Plans between UTMB & Western States

(75:50) Loose Plans as a Driver of Success

(79:20) Heat Preparation

(81:52) Thoughts on Kirsty Coventry’s Election

(83:21) Outro & Review

item.title: Longevity, Enhanced Games and Attention Spans
item.description:
Aside from the usual segments, this week we touch on some contract related issues in rugby, the enhanced games and Akani Simbine sparks a longevity and GOATs discussion. We then discuss some differences in soccer vs NFL and coaching style based on pressure. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:45) Doctor’s Question

(04:23) Standalone vs Ironman Marathon

(05:40) Hippocratic Oath

(07:41) Xylon’s Update 

(11:50) David’s Update

(15:20) Corrections

(18:35) Community Shoutouts

(20:05) Social Media Breaks

(22:10) Rugby Contracts 7s and 15s 

(27:00) Enhanced Games

(32:25) Akani Simbine, Longevity & GOATs

(39:48) Soccer vs NFL 

(43:10) Coaching Not to Lose

item.title: Rockstar Athletes
item.description:
In episode 64, Xylon and David discuss sleep and breathing, the need for tendons to be loaded rather than completely rested and then touch on some listener questions on rest post event and planning race nutrition. They then discuss On sponsoring the ITA, Grand Slam track’s changes and then quantification of the energy expenditure in an ultramarathon. The finish off with how to overcome shirt allergies and how to program for Hyrox.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:26) Medical Question

(02:00) David’s Training

(05:03) Xylon’s Update 

(12:45) Community Shoutouts

(14:51) Listener Question - Time off Post Event

(26:10) Listener Question - Race Nutrition Decision Making 

(31:34) Chris Froome Runs Parkrun  

(33:29) On Running Sponsors the ITA 

(38:58) Grand Slam Track Changes

(49:22) Energy Expenditure in Ultramarathons 

(56:16) Overcoming a Shirt Allergy 

(57:38) Hyrox Programming 

(67:12) What We’re Watching 

(68:08) Man Utd Chat

item.title: Wholistic Athletic Development
item.description:
We briefly touch on some community shoutouts, talk some hyrox and then review IM 70.3 St George as well as the Vuelta Espana Feminina before getting to this week’s interview. 

We are joined this week by Barry Austin who is a Level 3 UCI Certified Coach & UCI Certified Sporting Director. His career as a cycling coach and performance specialist spans more than two decades at both national and international levels. He has worked with a range of elite teams, including leading the South African National Cycling Team at the Olympic Games, and the UCI Women’s WorldTour team Canyon//SRAM Racing.

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:25) Doctor’s Question

(02:54) David’s Update

(03:10) Xylon’s Update

(06:40) David’s Running

(08:19) Hyrox

(14:34) The Psychology of Getting Back into Training

(18:19) Community Shoutouts

(23:21) Ironman 70.3 St George

(27:35) Vuelta Espana Feminina

(37:58) Interview Starts

(40:26) Barry’s System of Athlete Development 

(42:26) How Did Barry Develop his System? 

(46:19) Self Engagement 

(52:12) Losing Self Engagement 

(54:48) Training Self Engagement

(58:04) Training with Purpose 

(63:50) Buy in from Athletes

(66:27) Elemental Engagement 

(72:10) Progression of Elemental Engagement 

(74:00) What Separates Those Who Thrive in the Elements 

(76:50) Efficiency Patterns 

(80:50) Racecraft and Athletic Development

(88:35) Relational Engagement

(95:35) Pauline Ferrand-Prevot’s Performance  

(98:53) Learning to Win

item.title: Sustainability and Your Sport
item.description:
This week we are joined by Adjunct Professor Nicole Loher who is a climate scientist and communications expert to discuss an optimistic view on sustainability and sport. 

Nicole is the founder of C3 Climate Communications Collective and an Adjunct professor of climate communications at NYU. 

She holds a Bachelor's degree in marketing and communications from Fashion institute of technology and a Masters in climate change policy from columbia university. In her career she has worked with companies such as NARS cosmetics, Christian Louboutin, Microsoft and Hypergiant in roles across communications, AI and climate change. Beyond these roles she has also worked with brands such as Adidas and Tracksmith in the fitness space with her foray into triathlon and running. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(04:38) Cam Wurf’s Huge Intake at IM Texas

(07:57) Medical Question

(09:55) Interview Starts

(12:22) What is Nicole’s Role with FIFA

(14:55) What is Happening in Houston for the World Cup?

(17:39) Why is Sport the Starting Place for Sustainability? 

(21:55) What has Surprised Nicole about Working in Houston? 

(23:57) What are Some Campaigns that Nicole has Worked on for Houston? 

(26:47) How is Sports Handling Climate Change? 

(31:48) Is There Data on Temperature Changes in Sport? 

(32:53) Athletes’ Letter to the IOC on Climate Change

(36:47) How are Sports Handling Climate Change 

(43:53) Green Washing by Brands

(46:03) What Does Net 0 Mean? 

(50:54) What are the Chances of People Following Suit of Market Leaders

(52:03) Transport, Cities, Culture and Wealth Disparity 

(61:10) Actions that can be Taken

(62:52) Glass vs Plastic vs Aluminium

(64:50) Optimism in Climate

(66:10) Is AI the Solve for Climate?

(69:04) Review

(72:14) What are we watching? 



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner

item.title: Marathon Lessons
item.description:
Another running heavy episode incoming. After some updates and a bucketload of shoutouts, we answer a listener question about breathing when running. Following this we review London marathon and some things we can learn from that, followed by reviewing Ironman Texas before discussing Faith Kipyegon’s 4min mile attempt. We finish off with the doctor’s office as usual.   

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:26) Medical Question

(02:43) Xylon’s Update 

(09:53) David’s Update & Training

(16:56) Community Shoutouts

(26:10) Listener Question - Breathing When Running

(29:39) London Marathon 

(51:16) London Marathon Learnings

(53:30) Ironman Texas

(59:00) Faith Kipyegon’s 4 min mile Attempt 

(65:30) Doctor’s Office Sam Kerr

(66:28) What We’re Watching

item.title: When Should You Train?
item.description:
Before we get to our main topic of the episode, time of day and how to optimise training and performance around it, we discuss other happenings in sport. Specifically we discuss the Amstel Gold race, London Marathon field changes, Quade Cooper’s flag football goals and the Boston Marathon. We then finish with Patrick Lange in the Doctor’s office.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:42) Medical Question

(06:29) Exercise and Risks 

(15:46) David’s Update

(21:27) Xylon’s Update 

(26:10) Community Shoutouts

(30:53) Amstel Gold Race

(36:04) London Marathon Fields Thin out 

(38:23) Flag Football 

(41:15) Boston Marathon

(54:10) Puma Fast-R Nitro Elite 3 

(57:48) Evening Exercise and Sleep

(62:25) Time of Day and 10k Performance 

(68:10) Neuromuscular Performance and Time of Day

(68:55) Optimising Training & Performance with Time of Day

(74:53) Doctor’s Office Patrick Lange

(78:37) What We’re Watching 


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


David’s Newsletter Article He Mentioned

https://open.substack.com/pub/healthperformancenexus/p/whats-minimum-effective-dose

Whoop Study

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-58271-x 

Article on 10k TT and Time of Day

https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/07420528.2025.2459668 

Article on Time of Day on Neuromuscular Performance in Team Sports

https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/sports-and-active-living/articles/10.3389/fspor.2024.1466050/full

item.title: Personalised Carbohydrate Requirements - Tash Cooper-Smith
item.description:
We are joined by Tash Cooper-Smith from Precision Fuel & Hydration to discuss a paper she was an author on which covered the individualisation of carbohydrate requirements in athletes. Before we get to that, we cover a listener question and a tonne of community shoutouts before briefly discussing the Masters and Paris-Roubaix. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:05) Xylon’s Update 

(02:39) Exercise as Part of People’s Identity 

(06:05) David’s Alpine Time

(09:19) Medical Question

(10:41) Community Shoutouts

(16:03) Blood Donation Correction

(17:24) Listener Question - Tattoos and Autoimmunity

(22:53) Rory McIlroy Wins the Masters

(31:36) Paris-Roubaix

(39:23) Interview Starts

(41:25) 2025’s Bike Balance for Tash

(42:21) Office Rivalry Half Marathon

(42:58) What was Done in the Study?

(45:05) How was the Intensity of Exercise Chosen?

(46:09) How was Individual Carbohydrate Dose Determined? 

(47:57) Why Did the Topic Interest Tash?

(49:42) What Factors Influence Individual Differences in Carbohydrate Oxidation Rates?

(52:00) Gut Training

(52:35) Intensity as a Driver of Carbohydrate Need

(53:41) How Tash Increased her Carbohydrate Intake on Race Day

(55:50) Issues with Excessive Carbohydrate Intake

(56:40) Variability in Carbohydrate Oxidation Rates in the Study 

(59:35) Glucose vs Fructose

(62:24) Outcomes of the Study

(67:11) What is Upcoming for Tash

(71:16) Review 

(71:45) Doctor’s Office Wout van Aert

(72:34) What We’re Watching 


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


David’s Article on Tattoos

https://open.substack.com/pub/healthperformancenexus/p/tattoos

Article on Personalised Carbohydrate Dosing

https://performancenutrition.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s44410-025-00003-9

https://www.precisionhydration.com/performance-advice/nutrition/can-you-train-your-gut/

item.title: Blood Donation & Infrared Sauna’s Impacts on Performance
item.description:
On this week’s episode we discuss a fascinating listener question on blood donation and its impact on performance before reviewing some of the weekend’s sport and training of the elites heading to the London Marathon. We then look at a few papers on Infrared Sauna and Marathon Fueling before a quick discussion of Wout van Aert’s knee. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:50) Medical Fact Checking

(05:56) David’s Update

(11:05) Xylon’s Mood Swings

(14:55) Community Shoutouts

(26:15) Listener Question - Blood Donation 

(37:54) London Marathon Elite Training

(41:11) Training to Bridge IMSA and T100 Singapore

(45:16) IM 70.3 Oceanside

(49:25) Coming Back from an Achilles Injury 

(51:14) Grand Slam Track Thoughts 

(56:09) Tour of Flanders

(63:55) Infrared Sauna’s Impacts on Performance 

(70:06) Marathon Fueling and Performance 

(76:24) Doctor’s Office Wout van Aert

(79:22) What We’re Watching 



Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too):

item.title: Torpedo Bats and Placebo Supershoes
item.description:
Episode 57 is here and it brings all the regular segments and topics. We discuss some questions about pre race and marathon fueling as well as doing some community shoutouts. We move on to some happenings from IM South Africa and in the USADA world. Then we discuss an article about cyclists getting bigger before our main two topics; the torpedo bats the Yankees are using and a study examining the placebo effect of supershoes. We finish off in the doctor's office as usual. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:34) Medical Question

(02:55) Pre Marathon Snacks

(05:28) David’s Training

(10:34) Xylon’s Update

(16:27) Community Shoutouts

(18:25) Listener Question Marathon Questions

(23:14) Correction on Currency 

(23:48) IM South Africa Results 

(26:54) Shawnti Jackson Warned by USADA

(31:21) Cyclists Getting Heavier to Perform Better 

(33:33) Will We See Heavier Tour de France Finishers?

(37:55) Why Do Riders Have More Muscle These Days? 

(42:21) The Yankees Torpedo Bats 

(51:50) Supershoes and the Placebo Effect

(60:00) Doctor’s Office Fabio Jakobsen 

(64:00) What We’re Watching 


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner

https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19424280.2025.2458330#abstract

item.title: Only Fans, Coaches Behaving Badly & Stellar Performances
item.description:
Xylon is back! He and Dave cover a bunch of ground including reviewing the World Indoor T&F championships, Chianti by UTMB and Milan San Remo. They also discuss Kirsty Coventry’s election to IOC presidency, some coaches caught cheating and an athlete signing a contract with Only Fans. They finish off with some interesting folks in the doctor’s office and discussing Martin Van Riel’s crazy racing week upcoming. 


(00:00) Introduction

(01:22) Medical Question

(07:54) David’s Training

(12:02) Xylon’s Update

(17:57) Community Shoutouts

(35:33) The Boston Celtics are Sold

(54:43) World Indoor T&F Championships

item.title: That Running Episode
item.description:
Dave has a ring-in cohost in Xylon’s absence and this episode is quite running centric. Dave and guest host Dan review their recent race efforts with learnings, they then discuss fast running from Gout Gout, Jimmy Gressier and Jake Smith. They then touch on Eliud Kipchoge being used as a marketing tool, the Barkley Marathons and NYC Half Marathon results. Dave then interviews Rhodri Williams, who’s been running a mile for each point Wales lose by in the 6 Nations before Dan rejoins Dave for the doctor’s office featuring Xylon amongst others and they discuss a huge weekend upcoming in sport.

item.title: Rugby's Origins, Bifurcation and International Expansion
item.description:
After a brief chat about some recently cycling we are joined by journalist Mike Mehall Wood to discuss the origins of rugby league and rugby union, including the sociocultural factors that played into it, the week to week product and international expansion.

Mike is a rugby league journalist currently writing for Roar Rugby League. 

He has previously written for Forbes, VICE, LADbible, Dazed and HighSnobiety over a long career. He holds a degree in politics and cultural studies from the university of Leeds with a focus predominantly on the relationship between nationalism, ethnicity and culture.


(00:00) Introduction

(03:47) Classics Season

(12:42) Women’s Cycling Development and Depth

(27:53) Interview Starts

(30:20) What is a Cricket Tragic? 

(32:04) What is the Difference Between Rugby League and Rugby Union?

(38:10) Does Rugby Union Take Inspiration from Rugby League Rules?   

(39:46) Differences in Competitions Between Rugby League and Rugby Union

(45:53) How Did Rugby Union and Rugby League Split? 

(51:07) Have Any Players Transitions Between the Sports

(58:34) Why is There No Rugby League in South Africa? 

(61:20) Body Sizes in Rugby League and Rugby Union 

(63:05) What Are Salaries in Rugby League?

(64:56) International Rugby League

item.title: Birthdays, Tokyo Marathon & Doping Test Failures

item.title: Sodium Hyperhydration and Performance - Dr Sam Shepherd
item.description:
In this episode the guys discuss top earners in sport, some results from the track & field world and a drug ban. They then touch on some research and data on supershoes, specifically in trailrunning before being joined by Dr Sam Shepherd to discuss sodium hyperhydration and a paper on using it in women specifically.

(29:55) Carbon Plated Shoes in Trailrunning

(34:30) Supershoes Benefits in Trailrunning 

(37:29) Stack Height Effects on Gait

(57:48) What Does Sodium Hyperhydration Look Like in Practice? 

(61:04) The Importance of Practicing Hyperhydration

(65:29) Who Shouldn’t be using Hyperhydration?

item.title: Questioning Limiters in Endurance - Dr Andrew Koutnik
item.description:
Another week another bunch of running records which we cover. After this we chat a bit about Tarawera Ultramarathon results and the Tyreek Hill vs Noah Lyles showdown before touching on a new form of doping - Mitochondrial Transplants. 

We then get to an interview with Dr Andrew Koutnik a researcher  studying the influence of nutrition and metabolism on health, disease, and performance about a paper he was an author on; “Carbohydrate Ingestion Eliminates Hypoglycemia & Improves Endurance Exercise Performance in Triathletes Adapted to Very Low & High Carbohydrate Isocaloric Diets”


(00:00) Introduction

(07:17) David’s Heat Training

(10:15) Xylon Turns a Corner 

(14:00) Community Shoutouts 

(17:12) Indoor Track Records 

(21:30) New Half Marathon WR 

(26:16) Tarawera Results 

(29:50) Noah Lyles vs Tyreek Hill 

(32:40) Mitochondrial Transplants 

(40:30) Interview Starts

(42:40) Importance of Science Communication 

(46:02) What Was Done in the Study? 

(48:20) Timeline of Adaptation to Low Carb Diets

(53:05) Study Design 

(61:20) Findings of the Study 

(63:00) Study Population and Applicability 

(74:00) Metabolically Unhealthy Athletes 

(78:29) Outro and Review 

(84:40) Doctor’s Office 

(89:20) What We’ll Be Watching

item.dc:creator: 1% Better Podcast
item.itunes:summary:
Another week another bunch of running records which we cover. After this we chat a bit about Tarawera Ultramarathon results and the Tyreek Hill vs Noah Lyles showdown before touching on a new form of doping - Mitochondrial Transplants. 

We then get to an interview with Dr Andrew Koutnik a researcher  studying the influence of nutrition and metabolism on health, disease, and performance about a paper he was an author on; “Carbohydrate Ingestion Eliminates Hypoglycemia & Improves Endurance Exercise Performance in Triathletes Adapted to Very Low & High Carbohydrate Isocaloric Diets”


(00:00) Introduction

(07:17) David’s Heat Training

(10:15) Xylon Turns a Corner 

(14:00) Community Shoutouts 

(17:12) Indoor Track Records 

(21:30) New Half Marathon WR 

(26:16) Tarawera Results 

(29:50) Noah Lyles vs Tyreek Hill 

(32:40) Mitochondrial Transplants 

(40:30) Interview Starts

(42:40) Importance of Science Communication 

(46:02) What Was Done in the Study? 

(48:20) Timeline of Adaptation to Low Carb Diets

(53:05) Study Design 

(61:20) Findings of the Study 

(63:00) Study Population and Applicability 

(74:00) Metabolically Unhealthy Athletes 

(78:29) Outro and Review 

(84:40) Doctor’s Office 

(89:20) What We’ll Be Watching
