item.title: Training Models and Physiological Thresholds - Jem Arnold
item.description:
Xylon & David talk about some Millrose games results, national records falling in the 5k and Visma Lease a Bike’s use of TT helmets on the road before being joined by a guest to discuss training intensity distribution. 

Jem Arnold is a PhD candidate in exercise physiology at the University of British Columbia, where his research focuses on sport-related vascular conditions. He is also a physiotherapist, and endurance coach & consultant.


(00:00) Introduction

(06:18) David’s Training

(10:52) Xylon Being Grumpy 

(13:15) Millrose Games Results 

(18:15) Cassandre Beaugrand Breaking the French 5K Record 

(19:23) Visma Lease a Bike Using TT Helmets on the Road 

(22:55) Interview Starts

(24:40) What was the Paper Jem Contributed to? 

(26:52) The Role of Science Communication as a Researcher  

(28:47) What is Training Intensity Distribution? 

(34:26) What did the Research Group Do? 

(37:29) Which Sports Were Included? 

(37:57) Training Constraints and Periodisation 

(43:13) Reclassification of Participants in the Study

(48:08) Results of the Analysis 

(55:56) Thoughts on Why Different Levels of Athlete Respond Differently

(59:05) Day to Day Variation in Thresholds and Equipment Measurement Variability 

(70:30) Outro 

(70:55) Doctors Office 

(73:10) What we are Watching

item.title: Youth Development, Neuromuscular Fatigue & Breaking Records
item.description:
In episode 49 we cover a spate of running records being broken, some discussion about broadcast rights and the power of the individual in sports. We also discuss the UCI’s ban on repeated carbon monoxide inhalation before discussing athletic development in the contact of Albert Philipsen before we grab some audio from a conversation we had with his former coach about Albert. We finish off discussing ACL tears in the doctor's office. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:39) David’s Update 

(03:51) Adjusting Expectations as we Advance as Athletes

(10:08) Xylon’s Update

(13:30) Community Shoutouts

(16:30) Instrumented Footballs

(20:14) Grand Slam Track Broadcast Rights 

(22:51) Australian Rugby Broadcast Rights

(23:44) Records Tumble in the Running World 

(27:23) The Power of the Individual in Sport

(36:44) The Ban of Repeated Carbon Monoxide Inhalation by the UCI 

(39:39) Albert Philipsen’s Big Volume and Athletic Development

(45:20) Albert Philipsen’s Former Coach Gives Context for Albert 

(56:20) Neuromuscular Changes During the Women’s Football World Cup 

(61:40) Lisandro Martinez ACL Tear

item.title: Athletes vs Influencers
item.description:
Allison Lynch is a marketing and communications executive with 12 years of experience. This has included roles as Sr. Marketing Manager, US & Global Running and Coros and Senior marketing manager, running & women’s performance at Whoop. 

She joins us to discuss athletes vs influencers following a piece she wrote recently. 


(00:00) Introduction

(03:03) David’s Training

(06:00) Xylon’s Documentary Work

(11:52) Interview Starts

(13:10) How did Allison get into Marketing? 

(14:27) How did Allison End up Joining Whoop? 

(18:14) How did Allison Start Working with Coros? 

(20:58) What was Whoop’s Initial Strategy?

item.title: Estimating Carb & Calorie Burn and Machine Learning for Talent Prediction
item.description:
This week’s episode saw multiple listener questions answered including a discussion on prediction of caloric and carbohydrate burn from training data. There’s some quick fire talking points including the new TGL golf league, a women’s 5K world record and Netflix’s content strategy in sport. We then touch on the use of machine learning for talent prediction in football. Finally we look at injury and illness rates for the USA Olympic and Paralympic teams from the Tokyo games. 

 

(00:00) Introduction 

(03:41) David’s Update and Training

(06:23) Xylon’s Update 

(17:30) Community Shoutouts

(33:30) Listener Question - Training on Tired Legs

(36:10) Listener Question - Heart Rate’s Relationship to Calories 

(41:10) Predicting Carbohydrate and Caloric Burn

(46:50) Netflix Secures Women’s Football World Cup

(48:32) TGL Golf League 

(51:08) Women’s 5K World Record Broken  

(51:44) Ilona Maher Debuts for the Bristol Bears 

(54:04) The Coaching Roundabout 

(60:38) Machine Learning Prediction of Football Talent Selection

(64:20) Doctor’s Office - USA Tokyo Olympic & Paralympic Team Illness and Injuries

item.description:

item.title: Optimising Training During the Holidays
item.description:
In this Holiday Episode the guys discuss training during the holiday period and how to optimise it. In addition they cover a listener question on developing and quantifying durability as well as look at some research on faster marathoners habits and splitting up intervals like a Norwegian.

item.title: Does RED-S Exist? (Yes) - Assistant Professor Alex Coates
item.description:
Assistant Professor Alex Coates is the principal investigator at the “Exercise Physiology and Performance Lab” at Simon Fraser University. Her broad research interests cover applied exercise physiology, with a focus on understanding the impacts of varied exercise stimuli to systems level adaptations. This includes the areas of overtraining, under-fuelling, training adaptation, and monitoring techniques. She joined us to discuss a recent paper titled: “Does Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (REDs) Syndrome Exist?”


(00:00) Introduction

(04:45) David’s Training

(06:56) Alex Yee Running London Marathon

(11:31) Marco Arop’s Half Marathon Efforts

(12:00) Community Shoutouts 

(16:26) Interview Starts

(19:56) Overtraining and Overreaching

(26:25) How Can You Tell If You are Overreaching?  

(30:30) History of Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport 

(33:24) Alex’s Personal Experience

(43:38) What Does the Paper in Question Suggest? 

(45:43) The Allostatic Load Model

(48:16) Challenges with the Paper in Question

(54:29) Is There Utility in the Paper? 

(58:21) Disentangling Training and Nutrition Stressors

(64:53) Article Titles in Academia

(71:45) The Difference Between Overreaching and Underfueling

(76:15) The Takeaways from the Paper

(82:25) Outro and Review

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/57/17/1073 

https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/57/17/1098

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-024-02108-y

item.title: Supplements & the Supplement Industry with Grant Jenkins
item.description:
Grant Jenkins is the general manager of Propel Health, an Australian based supplement company. He is an exercise physiologist and ASCA Master Coach. He joins us to discuss the supplement industry, manufacturing, safe in sport accreditations and much more. We discuss supplement advice for endurance athletes, developing athletes and positive doping tests from tainted supplements. 


(00:00) Introduction

(05:35) Xylon’s Training Update

(07:33) David’s Training Update

(11:44) Community Shoutouts

(20:47) Interview Starts

(26:33) Black & White vs Grey Messaging 

(35:45) What Made Grant Start Businesses Outside of Physical Performance?

(39:11) What is a Naturopath? 

(41:56) What’s Required to Run a Supplement Company?

(44:22) Supplements for Endurance Athletes

(49:05) Supplements for Women vs Men 

(56:12) Can Supplements Help People with a Bad Lifestyle? 

(59:34) Contaminated Supplements and Positive Doping Tests

(65:57) Supplements for Developing Athletes

(73:45) Choosing a Protein Powder

(74:52) The Cost of Being Healthy

(77:20) The Protein Leverage Hypothesis 

(80:27) Skinny Fat 

(84:16) Review of Supplements 

(85:27) Nitrates 

(87:27) Protein Goals and Hitting Them

item.title: Swearing, Sleep & Failure Points
item.description:
In this episode the guys discuss some results from the Valencia marathon and address some listener questions about ultramarathon success and failure points. They go on to discuss swearing as a performance enhancer and the impact of orthorexia on sleep. 

 

(00:00) Introduction 

(04:00) David’s Update and Training

(05:13) Xylon’s Update and Birthday

(11:50) Community Shoutouts

(14:00) Valencia Marathon Observations

(16:38) How Can Someone Debut With Such a Fast Marathon? 

(21:35) Is the Success of East African Runners Good for Running? 

(24:24) Listener Question on Failure Points

(28:12) Listener Question on Muscle Damage in Ultramarathons

(38:38) World Athletics Awards

(42:27) Swearing as a Performance Enhancer

(46:32) Orthorexia and Sleep

(53:53) Doctor’s Office - Repeated Concussion

(57:56) December’s 1% Better Challenge

(61:25) What We are Watching

item.title: Gold Zones
item.description:
Paul Francis is the CEO and Cofounder of FC 32. Paul’s career includes time with both the Italian and British Olympic federations, multiple NFL teams and numerous advisory board positions including Strava. He was Global head of sports science for Adidas and prior to this he worked in innovation for nike. He joins us to discuss developing elite environments with lessons that span sport and business. 


(00:00) Introduction

(03:05) Interview Starts

(04:40) Gold Zones 

(06:17) Microdosing Excellence 

(06:45) How Does Paul’s System Fit into Scouting?

(08:08) Strengthening Weaknesses vs Strengthening Strengths 

(10:29) Stability as a Success Metric

(12:15) Game Style and Coach Selection

(14:25) Managing an Underperforming Player

(17:45) How Does the FC32 System Fit into Player Development 

(21:06) Coaching Incentives

(23:15) What Has Influenced Paul’s Outlook on Football Development 

(28:00) Following Fans vs Clubs  

(31:20) What Does the Initial Process of Taking Over Clubs Look Like?

(36:30) Communicating Decisions to Fans  

(38:55) How Does FC32 Choose Clubs? 

(41:41) How Does FC32 Work with Agents?

(44:41) What Role Does Mentorship Play in FC32?

(48:10) What are the Best Environments that Paul has Been in & What was Common Amongst Them? 

(53:12) Are Fans Less Patient These Days?

(56:14) Fans Influence on the Game 

(58:20) What’s the Biggest Level of Club FC32 Would Target? 

(61:30) November’s 1% Better Challenge


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner

⁠https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/16447373-the-gold-mine-effect⁠

item.title: Should Foreigners be Allowed to Coach National Teams?
item.description:
Hein Kriek is a Professional rugby coach, currently working as head coach of the Stellenbosch Academy of Sport Rugby International Institute. He has coached professionally in multiple countries and joins us to discuss coaching in foreign cultures following the recent appointment of Thoms Tuchel to coach the English Men’s Football team. There’s lots in this for non-rugby and football fans though, with many lessons on leadership and for the workplace.


(00:00) Introduction

(03:46) Interview Starts

(05:00) Coaching Future Springboks

(06:33) How Do We Know What Players Are Worth Financially?

(08:56) Off Field Factors That Impact Value in Rugby

(11:45) Differences Between Leagues in Value of Players

(14:29) Team Makeup and Player Profiles

(20:25) Mavericks in Sport

(22:34) Spotting and Nurturing Talent

(24:34) Challenges of Coaching in Foreign Cultures

(26:57) Difficulties of Coaching Overseas When Things Aren’t Going Well

(29:33) What Was The Appeal of Coaching in Different Cultures

(32:33) Lessons from Coaching in Different Cultures

(35:55) How Does a Coach Deal with Different Environments

(39:44) Leading in High Performance Environments

(47:52) Culture and Perspective

(51:22) Building the Foundation of Success

(61:21) A Crazy Game of Rugby

(63:34) Commonalities and Differences from Coaching Environments Hein has Been in

(67:01) Welcome to the Big League Moments

(68:23) Worst Loss Hein has Ever Had

(69:45) Moments that Make it Worth it

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: Predicting your Half Marathon Time, Caffeine's Impact on your Sleep and Updates to the 2025 WADA list
item.description:
Episode 37 covers some updates to WADA’s banned list, the impact of caffeine on sleep and half marathon time prediction. We also briefly discuss Grand Slam Track’s venue announcements, Neymar hits the doctor’s office and we cover a bunch of community shoutouts and listener questions. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:50) David’s Background  

(10:04) David’s Training

(12:00) Xylon’s Update

(17:50) Community Shoutouts

(22:48) Listener Question on Bone Stress Injuries

(25:30) Calcium Supplementation

(28:39) Listener Question on Kelvin Kiptum’s Potential

(36:00) Grand Slam Track Venues 

(38:59) Prediction of Half Marathon Times 

(46:35) Updates on WADA Banned List for 2025

(49:50) Caffeine and Impacts on Sleep 

(56:53) Doctor’s Office - Neymar 

(63:17) November’s 1% Better Challenge

item.title: Determinants of Endurance Performance
item.description:
This week the episode focuses on a listener question about the determinants of endurance performance. Before this, we briefly recap the New York Marathon and cover our usual segments including the Doctor’s Office where we touch on concussion, concussion protocols and CTE. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(06:00) David’s Recovery from Valencia Half Marathon 

(13:00) Community Shoutouts

(31:02) VO2Max and Longevity 

(33:57) Economy/Efficiency 

(37:14) Threshold 

(39:19) Critical Pace/Critical Power/FTP

(39:58) Lactate

(44:25) Durability/Fatigue Resistance 

(46:10) Factors Impacting Durability/Fatigue Resistance

item.title: Probiotics, AI Nutrition & Circadian Rhythm Affecting your Marathon
item.description:
This week’s episode starts with a recap of Cape Town Marathon and a view towards Valencia Half Marathon. We then tackle a listener question on potential downsides of heat training. After this we cover some thoughts from Chicago Marathon, some observations on brands in the running space and finally examine some papers on circadian rhythm and performance, AI vs dietitians and probiotic use. The Doctor’s Office has some research in it too this week, before we finish off with October’s 1% Better Challenge and a quick look at Kona. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:32) David’s Training Update 

(03:32) Traveling to Races 

(05:44) Xylon’s Cape Town Marathon Efforts

(19:13) Community Shoutouts 

(22:13) Listener Question on Drawbacks of Heat Training

(26:48) Chicago Marathon Observations

(30:27) Women’s Performances Nearing Men’s Performances  

(36:26) Parker Valby Signs with New Balance 

(40:43) Brands and Branding of Races 

(42:20) Probiotics GI Distress and Fatigue in Athletes  

(45:00) AI to Optimise Nutrition for Ultra Endurance 

(49:10) Circadian Rhythm, Sleep Inertia and Marathon Performance

(55:25) Doctor’s Office - Knee Injuries in The Premiership 

(59:00) October’s 1% Better Challenge

(61:27) What We are Watching

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/sports-and-active-living/articles/10.3389/fspor.2024.1386627/full

Article on Probiotics and Fatigue

https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/15502783.2024.2388085#d1e1219 

Article on AI in Ultraendruance Nutrition 

https://www.termedia.pl/Optimizing-athletic-performance-through-advanced-nutrition-r-nstrategies-can-AI-and-digital-platforms-have-a-role-in-ultraendurance-sports-,78,54384,1,1.html

https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jsr.14375

item.title: Preparing for Kona with Pro Triathlete Matthew Marquardt
item.description:
Matthew Marquardt is a professional triathlete currently Ranked 19th in the PTO rankings and 9th in the IM Pro Series. He came 11th in the 2023 IM World Championships and joins us to talk about his preparation for Kona 2024 and how he balances being a professional triathlete with being a full time medical student. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:24) The Heart, its Function and Adaptation to Training

(10:07) David Balancing Recovery, Training and Freshness 

(13:30) Xylon’s Health and Cape Town Marathon

(18:27) Community Shoutouts 

(22:15) Interview Starts

(25:28) Matthew’s Run Technique Work

(32:26) How has Matthew’s Training Been Going? 

(34:00) IM Pro Series

(37:15) Matthew’s Training Week and Balancing it with Study

(41:28) No Longer Doing Brick Sessions

(45:30) How has Matthew’s Training Changed Since he was an Amateur?

(49:20) How does Matthew think Kona Will Play Out? 

(52:25) What does Success Look Like for Matthew at Kona? 

(53:30) Matthew’s Nutrition Plan

(55:30) Matthew’s Heat Preparation for Kona

(60:30) Matthew’s Shoe Choices and use of Supershoes

(65:40) Lactate and What Impacts it

(69:29) Matthew’s Caffeine Strategy

(74:44) September 1% Better Challenge

(75:55) What We’ll Be Watching

item.title: The Business of Football & Revolutionising the Women's Game with Christie Jenkins
item.description:
We are joined on the podcast by Christie Jenkins, Co-Founder & Chief Investment Officer at F32 a multi-club football ownership group focused on world class player development. We discuss her journey, including competing in 3 sports at an international level and background in finance. We also discuss Women's sport and what the future looks like, including FC32’s approach to this.

item.title: Underfueling in Football, Carb Requirements and body weight, IMWC & Pogacar
item.description:

item.title: Competing Safely in Endurance Events
item.description:
Dr Amy Boalch joins the podcast this week to talk about safe participation in endurance events and common problems seen by event medical staff. Amy is the project lead for the race ready app, a medical doctor and currently undertaking her masters in medical education. She shares best practice advice for how to hydrate and manage pain in endurance events as well as some mistakes she sees in athletes preparing for races.

item.title: Interference Effect, Carbohydrates for Recovery & Physiology of Backyard Ultras
item.description:
This week the community really shone and we answer a number of listener submitted questions, some about previous episodes some not. From there we discuss Jakob Ingebrigtsen’s debut half marathon and then it is onto some scientific papers. One on the physiology of backyard ultras and the other on recovery and carbohydrates. We finish up with some concussion discussion in the doctor’s office.   

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:26) Xylon’s Pain in the Butt

(08:12) David’s Using Shoes to Help his Training 

(09:41) Xylon’s Shoe Shopping and Shoe Size

(13:09) Blister Issues and Solving Them

(16:36) Community Shoutouts

(19:29) Measuring Magnesium Levels

(21:49) Should you Split your Strength and Endurance Training up or do Them on the Same day? 

(25:05) Should you Vary your Running Terrain?

(28:40) How Transferable is Fitness Across Sports?

(39:01) Jakob Ingebrigtsen’s Half Marathon Debut 

(43:24) The Physiology of “Backyard Ultras” 

(51:00) Carbohydrate Refueling

(54:50) Doctor’s Office - Concussion

(58:25) September 1% Better Challenge

(59:50) What We are Watching


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner



Article on Magnesium

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4455825/#:~:text=Magnesium%20further%20modulates%20insulin%20signal,function%20of%20proteins%20and%20mitochondria.

Article on Concussion and the Fencing Response

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8922760/

Paper on Backyard Ultra Physiology 

https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ejsc.12190 

Video of Tua’s Concussion 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QXdxpRCaR0k

item.title: Adapting to Training
item.description:
This week is all about adapting to training. The guys discuss not ice bathing post training, longitudinal changes due to sprint training, life long cardiac changes in an elite marathon runner and the use of power data in anti doping. They also touch on magnesium supplementation and the race between Mondo Duplantis and Karsten Warholm, which landed the latter in the doctor’s office. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:06) Why You Shouldn’t Ice Bath After Training 

(03:17) Xylon’s Training and Rationale 

(08:44) Value of Consistency 

(14:00) David’s Training Turning a Corner

(20:17) Magnesium Use in Endurance Athletes

(28:24) Different Magnesium Types

(33:10) Community Shoutouts

(40:40) Should Power Data Be Added to Anti Doping in Cycling? 

(49:03) Season Adaptations to Sprinting

(52:13) Longitudinal Adaptations to Elite Marathon Running and Heart Problems

(63:54) Doctor’s Office and Sprinting 

(67:10) September 1% Better Challenge

(69:00) What We are Watching


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner



Article on Using Power as Part of Anti Doping 

https://journals.humankinetics.com/view/journals/ijspp/aop/article-10.1123-ijspp.2024-0088/article-10.1123-ijspp.2024-0088.xml 


Article on Changes in Muscle as a Result of Sprint Training

https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ejsc.12155 


Article on Longitudinal Changes in Marathon Runner

https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/japplphysiol.00070.2024?rfr_dat=cr_pub++0pubmed&url_ver=Z39.88-2003&rfr_id=ori%3Arid%3Acrossref.org

item.title: The Future
item.description:
In our episode this week the guys discuss some of the changing landscape of sport; be it the body changing in response to strongman, the evolution of trailrunning, projects vs racing or U20 progression through to open racing. They briefly discuss the use of exogenous ketones, the benefits of group training and of course debut September’s 1% better challenge.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:35) Exogenous Ketone Use 

(01:23) What are Ketones?

(06:00) Research on Exogenous Ketones

(11:22) David’s Struggles with his Running

(15:04) RPE Whilst Training with Others

(17:29) Xylon’s Training

(23:47) Community Shoutouts

(27:25) Impacts of Strongman Training on the Body

(35:52) U20 World T&F Championships and Performance Projection 

(43:05) Killian Jornet’s “Alpine Connections” Project

(47:39) UTMB Observations

(51:18) Women vs Men and Recovery

(55:34) Ruth Croft’s Race and Pacing

(59:27) The Attrition of the Men’s Race at UTMB

(61:42) Suguru Osaka’s Running at UTMB

(63:49) Diversity in Trailrunning

(67:00) The Widening Gap Between Have’s & Have Not’s in Sport

(69:28) Doctor’s Office 

(73:50) September 1% Better Challenge


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: If it’s Not Doping What is it?
item.description:
In addition to answering a listener question about the impact of technology on sprinting, this week we discuss the use of theming in coaching. We also talk about the NYC marathon elite field, with many backing up from the Olympics and implications for this and we look at a paper quantifying the impact of steroid use. In response to requests we also discuss marathon training for Xylon in a little more depth. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(03:03) Xylon’s Cape Town Marathon Preparation 

(08:00) Not Racing Your Training Sessions 

(10:10) Lessons from the Best in the World

(16:07) David’s Training and Shoe Choices

(18:37) Training in Race Shoes

(22:17) Community Shoutouts 

(24:17) Adam Peaty’s Road to Paris, Theming & Storytelling   

(32:03) NYC Marathon Elites Fields and People Backing up from the Olympics 

(37:56) How Much do Steroids Improve Performance? 

(46:19) Listener Question - Benefits of New Spikes to Sprint Events

(69:20) August 1% Better Challenge

(70:04) What We’re Watching


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: Muscle Memory & Immunity
item.description:
In our 25th weekly episode, we cover some crazy performances from the sporting world before diving into some research on immunity with nutrition. We then touch on muscle memory and some of its implications for doping. As usual we finish with a look at the Doctor’s office, the 1% Better Challenge for August and what we will be watching. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:48) David’s Training for Cardiff Half Marathon

(05:24) Xylon’s Marathon Training  

(10:11) Progression in Training and What to Expect

(16:05) Community Shoutouts 

(17:20) Tour de France Femmes Final Stage Insanity 

(21:07) Kristian Blummenfelt’s Win at Frankfurt Ironman

(23:32) What is Durability and Fatigue Resistance? 

(25:11) Why Did Patrick Lange Have HIs Shoes in a Cooler? 

(28:00) Carbohydrate Intake and Immunity

(34:33) Does Muscle Memory Exist? 

(44:41) Do the Effects of Doping Last a Lifetime? 

(50:00) The Doctor’s Office 

(55:42) August 1% Better Challenge

(57:26) What We’re Watching

We then touch on muscle memory and some of its implications for doping. As usual we finish with a look at the Doctor’s office, the 1% Better Challenge for August and what we will be watching. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:48) David’s Training for Cardiff Half Marathon

(05:24) Xylon’s Marathon Training  

(10:11) Progression in Training and What to Expect

(16:05) Community Shoutouts 

(17:20) Tour de France Femmes Final Stage Insanity 

(21:07) Kristian Blummenfelt’s Win at Frankfurt Ironman

(23:32) What is Durability and Fatigue Resistance? 

(25:11) Why Did Patrick Lange Have HIs Shoes in a Cooler? 

(28:00) Carbohydrate Intake and Immunity

(34:33) Does Muscle Memory Exist? 

(44:41) Do the Effects of Doping Last a Lifetime? 

(50:00) The Doctor’s Office 

(55:42) August 1% Better Challenge

(57:26) What We’re Watching 


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/sports-and-active-living/articles/10.3389/fspor.2024.1264814/full

item.title: GOATs and Super Subs
item.description:
Our focus this week is on people who have GOAT arguments and the super subs who stepped up at the Olympics. We also discuss Olympic medals and happiness as well as discussing some listener questions, our 1% Better challenge for August and a visit to the Doctor’s Office. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:56) David’s polarized training in Chamonix 

(02:08) Xylon’s Cape Town Marathon Training 

(03:08) The Impact of Travel, Walking & Heat on Performance in Training  

(06:00) Xylon’s Sweat Test 

(09:36) Community Shoutouts, Listener Question on Recovery Supplements

(14:08) Ice Baths and Recovery Boots for Recovery 

(16:08) Optimising vs Adapting

(20:47) Listener Question Omnius Headbands

(25:00) Medal Winners and Happiness

(32:12) GOATs 

(32:25) Killian Jornet Winning Zegama Strengthening his GOAT Status

(39:14) Tia-Clar Toomey-Orr’s Record Setting 7th Crossfit Games Win 

(42:07) GOAT Showdown in the Marathon Bekele vs Kipchoge 

(45:33) Sifan Hassan & Helen Obiri’s GOAT Arguments 

(51:08) Faith Kipyegon’s Insane Achievement 

(54:03) Super Subs - Performances of Olympic Reserves 

(57:55) The Doctor’s Office 

(59:53) August 1% Better Challenge

(61:29) What We’re Watching

item.title: Everything you need to know about the women's Tour de France
item.description:
Show Notes:

In this bonus episode, we talk to Hannah Walker. Hannah is a sports broadcaster and commentator who specializes in cycling.

She has covered all of the major tours, including being one of the inaugural commentators for the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift. 

In addition to this she has commentated on the World Championships and was the lead commentator and podium announcer for the commonwealth games. 

Hannah formerly worked as communications, PR and Social media manager for the CERATIZIT - WNT Pro Cycling and was a professional cyclist for team WNT-Rotor Pro Cycling.


Follow Hannah here: https://www.instagram.com/spannawalker/

(00:00) Introduction

(01:35) The history of the women’s Tour de France 

(03:15) Hannah Walker joins us

(04:30) How do the men’s and women’s TDF differ?

(06:32) How are they doing 8 stages over 7 days

(15:26) Split stage of day 2 

(17:40) Why is Grace Brown retiring?

(22:15) There are big name transfers coming

(28:14) Stage 4 moves into Belgium and hills

(37:25) Who are the GC leaders

(48:25) Teams who won’t target GC

(54:30) Why will Alpe d’Huez be special?

Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: Interesting observations from the Olympics
item.description:
Show Notes:

This week’s episode see’s Xylon recounting his experience at the Paris Olympic Games while David flew to London to run with a gold medalist. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:36) David’s crazy last 24 hours

(03:00) Xylon’s travels are becoming exorbitant

(03:43) Xylon was in Paris at the opening night of the athletics

(04:25) Shoutout to a silver medalist we have a loose connection to

(09:20) Swapping Olympic sports

(10:00) David met silver medalist Beth Potter

(11:26) Why rowing may have helped Kristian Faulkner

(15:10) Taylor Knibb only USA athlete to compete in Paris in two different sports

(18:14) Two Olympic appearances 16 years apart

(21:58) Athletes receiving private funding to the Olympics

(31:40) The most followed rugby player in the world

(33:30) An athlete's private parts stopping him from winning

(39:22) Good and bad product placements at the Olympics

(44:58) Xylon doesn’t like what Nike did at the Olympics

(46:59) Why are there so many hamstring injuries in Paris?

(50:30) August 1% Better Challenge

item.title: Preparing a Crossfit Athlete
item.description:

(38:54) Elite Crossfit vs Using Crossfit

(45:57) Performing at the Crossfit Games, Recovery and Preparation for it

(56:00) How Can a Great Endurance Athlete be Weak in the Gym?  

(62:00) Review of Interview

item.title: How African cycling can become a global player
item.description:
Off the back of Biniam Girmay’s historic performance at the Tour de France, Xylon and David discuss African cycling as a means to further discuss talent identification, cultivation and cultural challenges in sport. They also chat to professional cyclist Merhawi Kudus, one of the first black Africans (along with Daniel Teklehaimanot) to ride in the Tour de France, about African cycling, Biniam and what needs to be done to have better representation in the peloton. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:30) David’s Running in Chamonix

(02:50) HRV Learnings

(04:48) Xylon’s Recent Running 

(06:29) Why Intensity Control is so Important in Endurance Training

(08:57) Community Shoutouts

(12:03) Interview with Merhawi Kudus

(13:33) Where did Merhawi’s Inspiration to Become a Cyclist come from? 

(14:43) When did Merhawi Realise he could become a Professional Cyclist? 

(15:20) What is life like in a UCI Cycling Center? 

(16:10) What was it like for Merhawi going to Europe? 

(17:37) How was it for Merhawi Adapting to European Culture? 

(19:15) Who were the other Riders in the Center with Merhawi?  

(19:54) What Kept Merhawi Going When Others Gave up?  

(21:29) What was Merhawi’s Experience like as a black African in the Peloton?  

(23:22) Does Merhawi have Younger Riders Reaching out to him as a Role Model?  

(23:50) When did Merhawi First meet Biniam Girmay?  

(24:39) How did Merhawi Feel Seeing Biniam Performing so well at the Tour de France this Year?  

(26:51) Are Most Eritrean Riders Climbers?  

(27:41) Will Biniam’s Achievements Open the Door for African Cycling?  

(29:30) Does Merhawi Think Mentorship of Young African Cyclists is Important in the Context of Elite Cycling Team Staff?  

(30:20) How is Professional Cycling Changing at the Moment?

(32:03) Does Merhawi Worry that Africa Could Fall Further Away Given the Advancements in Technology in Cycling? 

(33:26) Does Merhawi Think we need more Racing in Africa?

(35:04) What Does Merhawi think Hosting the World Championships will mean for Africa?

(37:29) Thought off the Back of Biniam’s Victory  

(39:21) Will Biniam’s Victory Mean we see Significant Progress and more Opportunities? 

(42:21) Talent Identification 

(44:23) Challenges with Access to Talent Identification Opportunities

(46:00) Misaligned Incentives in Talent Identification in Cycling and Examples of Better Alignment in Other Sports 

(49:12) Academy Systems and Culturally Appropriate Solutions 

(52:20) Why Cultural Diversity is Important in Sport 

(57:51) Why are there Not More East African Cyclists?

(58:55) The Importance of Role Modelling and Heros in Sport

(62:05) The Physiology of African Cyclists 

(63:45) The Progress Xylon has Seen in Developing African Cyclists

(64:51) Development Structures 

(66:48) Cultural Integration Challenges 

(70:45) Cultural Diversity and Development Structures 

(75:22) Talent Identification Systems and the Academy & Institute Systems 

(82:00) Xylon’s Work in Cycling in Africa

(83:24) The July 1% Better Challenge

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

item.title: Is Carbon Monoxide Performance Enhancing and Should it Be Banned?
item.description:
In this episode we cover peaking in sport, whether going to live sporting event is good for your health and changes to the qualifying through the rounds of the Olympic Track Events. We then quickly visit the doctor’s office which is full of Sprinboks before discussing Carbon Monoxide in sport, how it works, is it performance enhancing and should it be banned? 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:34) Xylon’s Trivia Escapades

(04:27) Community Shoutouts

(05:29) Peaking and Age of Performance at the Olympics

(19:52) Could Usain Bolt have Won Another Olympic Medal?

(22:31) Live Event Attendance and Wellness

(25:29) Olympic Track Event Qualification and Repechage System

(31:00) Doctor’s Office 

(32:44) Carbon Monoxide use in the Peloton 

(40:11) How does Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing Work to Measure Haemoglobin? 

(44:55) Why Would You Use Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing?

(46:14) How Much Does Haemoglobin Increase Due to Altitude? 

(47:51) Why May Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing be an Issue? 

(49:00) What is the Role of Carbon Monoxide in the Body?

(50:35) Can Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing be Used for Training?

(51:46) Articles on Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing Use for Performance

(57:01) Is Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing Doping and/or Will it be Banned? 

(57:31) How Does the Athlete Biological Passport Work?

(60:30) How Does a Substance or Method Make it Onto the Banned List? 

(61:25) Should Carbon Monoxide Rebreathing be Added to the Banned List? 

(64:41) July’s 1% Better Challenge

item.title: Crazy Running and Olympic Selection
item.description:
This week has a heavy focus on the Olympics and running. The guys touch a bit on Jonas Abrahamsen’s story of weight gain for performance gain before transitioning to discuss Olympic selection, Sifan Hassan’s crazy schedule and then some crazy running at Paris Diamond League and at Challenge Roth. It’s July so they also have a new 1% Better Challenge for you.

item.title: Myocarditis with Professional Triathlete Eloise du Luart
item.description:
David briefly reviews Lavaredo and shares some strange things he saw and Xylon’s not running as much as he thought. They then discuss race medals, and some community shoutouts. They cover some happenings at the Tour de France, the crazy fast day at Western States 100 and then return to the second half of the interview with Eloise du Luart covering her Myocarditis and how things progressed through the process. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:34) Race shirts and Medals

(03:17) David’s Lavaredo Recap and Observations 

(11:00) Xylon’s Running  

(12:58) Community Shoutouts 

(16:00) Quickfire Happenings in Sport  

(18:49) Jan Hirt’s Broken Teeth

(21:15) Batman Sunglasses and the UCI Banning them 

(23:00) The UCI and Concussion

(28:11) Biniam Girmay Making History  

(33:24) Western States 100 Results & Strength of Field  

(39:31) The Doctor’s Office  

(41:18) Eloise’s Big Setback  

(49:13) Eloise’s Time in CCU  

(53:53) How Long Was Eloise is Hospital and What Was Eloise’s Diagnosis 

(57:42) Eloise’s Coronary Angiogram Experience

(60:33) A Second Incident and Second Angiogram

(63:50) Being Discharged to Return for an MRI 

(65:45) Confirmation of a Diagnosis and Treatment Plan 

(60:50) Return to Training 

(73:38) How is Eloise’s Headspace During Training Now 

(78:20) Post Injury Return to Sport  

(82:31) Low Heart Rates in Athletes in Hospital

item.title: Technology and Triathlon - pro triathlete Eloise du Luart
item.description:
Race week for David looms as Xylon gets back into training. The guys discuss Christiano Ronaldo’s longevity and what the world was like when he debuted, alongside some interesting ethical considerations in the world of doping in track and field. The finish off with part one of an interview with professional triathlete Eloise du Luart about her use of technology in helping her solve some performance problems. 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:00) David’s Lavaredo Preparations

(03:39) David’s Lavaredo Nutrition Plan 

(07:08) David’s Taper for Lavaredo 

(10:00) Navigating Problems on Race Day

(14:13) How Much Time Should you have off Post Race  

(20:00) What Events does Xylon have Coming up? 

(24:53) Community Shoutouts 

(26:21) Christiano Ronaldo’s Longevity and Factors Contributing to it

(30:33) Hard work vs Talent

(31:52) Dennis Mitchell as a Coach Previously Serving a Doping Ban

(37:28) Justin Gatlin Competing Post Doping Ban

(38:28) Physiology vs Morals & Ethics in Doping

(41:18) The Banned Substance List

(44:18) Interview Starts

(46:54) Eloise’s Background 

(47:40) The Training vs Racing Disconnect

(50:46) The Challenge of Racing not Reflecting Training

(54:20) Using Technology to Solve Performance Problems

(61:42) Time Trial Bike Position Issues 

(73:32) Bonus Episodes 

(74:49) Xylon and Dave’s TdF Picks

(75:47) June’s 1% Better Challenge

(76:33) What we Will be Watching

(77:25) David’s Thoughts on Western States 100mi Favourites

item.title: Tour de France preview - with expert commentator Hannah Walker
item.description:
Hannah Walker is a sports broadcaster and commentator who specialises in cycling. She has covered all of the major tours, including being one of the inaugural commentators for the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift. 

In addition to this she has commentated on the World Championships and was the lead commentator and podium announcer for the commonwealth games. 

Hannah formerly worked as communications, PR and Social media manager for the CERATIZIT - WNT Pro Cycling and was a professional cyclist for team WNT-Rotor Pro Cycling.


(00:00) Introduction

(01:00) Tour de France for dummies 

(01:30) How many teams in the Tour de France

(03:45) What is a breakaway and how is it formed

(06:40) How are teams built

(09:20) Can you win the TDF without winning a stage  

(24:40) Why is the leaders jersey yellow

(27:00) What’s exciting Hannah this year

(34:21) Breaking news out of the Visma camp

(41:50) Who gets to be GC leaders

(47:20) What will GC look like after week one

(51:40) What does week 2 look like

(56:56) Who are the best sprinters 

(61:05) Who will be Hannah’s breakout star

(71:11) Why Matej Mohorič is a genius 


Follow Hannah on Instagram and X:

https://www.instagram.com/spannawalker/

https://x.com/spannawalker

item.title: Is Changing Sports the Future of Endurance Athletes?
item.description:
Xylon’s sick and David explains why post event sickness happens and what you can do to prevent it all as he is tapering for Lavaredo. They then discuss how to maintain performance when injured, the benefits of course knowledge and changes to the Tour de France course. Following this they discuss Michael Johnson’s Grand Slam Track, the Spring Energy gel drama and the Euros. Finally they touch on choking before getting onto the main topic of the episode: repurposing of endurance sport talent and athletes changing sports. A new segment is also debuted; “what we will be watching” where Xylon and David highlight some upcoming sport they’ll be keeping an eye on. 

 

(00:00) Introduction

(00:25) Xylon’s Illness 

(01:03) Post Race Illness, Why it Happens and How to Prevent it

(03:37) Recovery, Social Media and Sleep

(04:51) David’s Training and Now Tapering

(05:51) Community Shoutouts

(07:21) Strategies to Maintain Performance Whilst Injured 

(11:08) Why did Lucy Charles Barclay do Ironman Nice?

(13:12) Coeliac Disease and Being an Athlete

(14:06) Course Changes to the Tour de France 

(17:57) Grand Slam Track 

(27:34) The Spring Energy Gel Saga 

(33:07) Euro 2024 

(35:24) Euro 2024’s Use of Technology & Rule Changes 

(40:16) Rory McIlroy’s Choking and What Is Choking? 

(46:23) Kylian Mbappe Enters the Doctor’s Office 

(49:22) Repurposing Talent & Endurance Athletes Switching Sports 

(60:30) June 1% Better Challenge

(61:35) What We Will Be Watching

item.title: Is Exercising Outdoors Better For You?
item.description:
Xylon and David cover indoor vs outdoor training in the main portion of this episode after touching on all the regular segments. Usain Bolt enters the doctor’s office, and the guys discuss some NCAA track & field, tennis, skateboarding and cycling.   

 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:17) Recovery Discussion

(06:52) David’s Lavaredo Preparation

(11:12) Community Shoutouts

(13:51) Parker Valby’s Recent Successes and Low Mileage Training

(17:12) Dauphine Results and What it Means for the Tour de France

(25:18) The First Female 900 in Skateboarding

(28:08) Carlos Alcaraz Being the Youngest Man to Ever Win on all 3 Surfaces

(29:35) Tactical Aspects of Tennis

(30:56) Iga Swiatek

(33:10) Usain Bolt Visits The Doctor’s Office

(38:44) Indoor vs Outdoor Training

(44:50) Is Indoor Training Harder?

(49:32) Are the Benefits to Exercising in Nature?

(50:10) What are the Benefits of Outdoor Training?

(51:44) Why May Mixing Indoor and Outdoor Training Be Helpful?

(54:40) Examples of Indoor Training Success

(56:40) June’s 1% Better Challenge

item.title: Comrades Marathon Learnings and Advice
item.description:
In this bonus episode David and Xylon dissect and recap Xylon's Comrades Marathon effort. We dive into learnings, feedback, thoughts and advice for those planning to run the Comrades or do any endurance event.


(00:00) Introduction

(01:18) Post race sleep and vibe

(02:01) Supporting and crewing vs racing

(02:30) Race Debrief

(06:54) Nightmare happenings on race morning

(08:37) Xylon’s pacing buddy

(09:27) Climbing in Comrades

(11:13) Comradery in Comrades

(13:06) Xylon’s position movement through the race

(16:25) The fallacy of banking time early

(17:21) Racing the person you ran your whole race with

(18:55) Xylon’s pace splits

(19:46) Xylon’s cramping history

(21:03) Comrades fueling strategy

(28:56) Lessons for planning your fueling

(34:58) Comrades course lessons

(37:12) Recovery from ultras and decision making

(38:23) Xylon’s thoughts on training and what he’d do next year

(42:31) Xylon’s advice to first time Comrades runners

(45:14) How do you get home after the Comrades

(46:26) How does Xylon feel today

(47:47) Xylon’s thoughts on his heat preparation

item.title: Trending in Sport
item.description:
Xylon and David cover trends in sport this week. We discuss tapering in light of Xylon’s Comrades taper, recovery from major events and Xylon’s fueling plan for the Comrades. We then answer a listener question and get to what’s come across David’s desk including the announcement of World Athletics Ultimate Championship and a discussion about changing coaches in sport and injury plagues. We then go on to discuss America’s history in cricket of all things! Following this we touch on the new Unrivaled 3 on 3 basketball league and the historic agreement for college’s to pay college players before serving up this month’s 1% better challenge.  

 

(00:00) Introduction

(01:01) The Art and Science of Tapering

(07:30) Recovery post major event

(12:10) David running in 4 countries in a week

(14:50) Xylon’s Comrades fueling

(16:34) Community Shoutouts

(18:08) Listener Question about breaking up continuous episode

(25:38) World Athletics Ultimate Championship

(32:12) Changing of Coaches/Managers

(39:20) Injury Plagues in Sport

(44:00) Cricket in America

(48:34) America’s History in Cricket

(50:49) The Unrivalled Basketball League

(54:27) NCAA and Power 5 Agree to a Deal that Let Schools Pay Players

(60:00) June’s One Percent Better Challenge

item.title: Training and Performing in the Heat - Dr Jamie Stanley
item.description:
Hot off the press, this episode is all about heat and heat training with guest, Dr Jamie Stanley. We briefly catch up about training and some potential upcoming topics before we dive straight into our interview.

item.title: So Good They Changed the Rules and Breaking the Rules
item.description:
This week is all about rules. Some athletes and teams are good enough to make the sport change them, others break them and some sports are evolving and changing them. We also have our regular segments including our 1% Better Challenge and some listener questions. 

(00:00) Introduction

(02:20) David’s Brooklyn Half Marathon Efforts

(04:32) Xylon’s Training and Carrying Fatigue

(07:07) Comrades Marathon Programs and Taper

(09:43) Community Shoutouts

(10:47) Recovery, Inflammation and Ice Baths

(15:10) Barefoot Training and Foot Strength

(18:40) World Rugby Law Changes

(24:05) Sport’s Changing the Rules Because of Dominance of an Individual

(27:34) Taylor Knibb’s Qualification for a Second Olympic Event

(31:30) A Businessman Paying Prize Money to a Boston Marathon Winner who Finished behind someone found to be Doping

(35:00) UTMB World Series Drug Testing Athletes

(44:54) How Much do the Top Trail Runners Earn?

(47:30) The FA Preventing Heading in Youth Football/Soccer

(54:23) The May 1% Better Challenge

item.title: How Many Running Shoes do you Need?
item.description:
This week’s episode is a big one on shoes and choosing shoes. We touch on a listener question about nutrition during taper before diving into our interview with our guest Michal ‘Nitta’ Nitschke. He joins us and explains all things choosing shoes, injury risk and how shoes have evolved. 

(00:00) Introduction 

(00:24) Xylon complains about heat training 

(01:35) Dave’s Running Plans

(03:57) Community Shoutouts

(05:13) Nutritional Intake During Taper

(10:30) Interview Starts

(12:16) How did Michael get into sport

(14:28) How did Michael end up in podiatry 

(17:10) What was Michael’s first running shoe 

(18:21) Recreational runners and injury risk factors (21:04) The role of devices as run mileage increases

(22:08) What evolution have we seen in shoes? 

(26:34) How do you choose running shoes? 

(32:09) How do you select a second running shoe in addition to your first pair? 

(36:06) The biggest technological advancement in footwear in they last 2 decades 

(37:40) Footprint surface area and its importance

(38:43) How do you choose a third shoe if you want one? 

(40:40) Examples of some shoes in different categories 

(42:26) Two different ways to use a second shoe 

(43:01) How long do shoes last?

(47:27) Is it wise to just buy the next version of your current shoe? 

(50:44) Should you be training in supershoes? 

(57:40) What are some features to look for in a shoe when injured? 

(61:58) Should you run the Comrades in a supershoe?

(65:35) Michael’s marathon training for his 2:33 

(68:30) What creates injuries in runners?

(71:02) The 4 F’s of footwear 

(71:52) The decision about a second shoe as a key one 

(73:53) 1% Better Challenge

Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Michael’s Instagram, Strava and Practice Website.

item.title: Nicotine Use in Sport - Saint or Zynner?
item.description:
This week we discuss the health benefits of mass participation sporting events and their risks, we touch on Kylian Mbappe racing Usain bolt and finally Red Bull’s acquisition of Bora-Hansgrohe before we talk about the use of nicotine in sports in response to a listener question. 

(00:00) Introduction 

(01:54) Xylon going Rogue on his Training

(05:15) Chicken Liver and it’s Health Benefits  

(07:08) David’s Trip in the Alps 

(09:16) Community Shoutouts 

(10:31) Listener Question about Heat Training and CORE Temperature Sensor 

(18:00) Benefits and Risks of Mass Participation Sporting Events 

(23:50) Why Would an Arrhythmia mean that someone needs to Stop a Professional Sporting Career

(26:54) Red Bull Buying Bora-Hansgrohe 

(31:24) Kylian Mbappe racing Usain Bolt 

(35:50) Doctors Office, Emma Coburn’s Broken Ankle 

(39:18) Nicotine in Sports - Listener Question 

(39:54) How does Nicotine Work?

(41:24) What are the Forms of Nicotine that People can use?

(43:11) What are the Benefits of Nicotine?

(45:58) What are the Risks of Nicotine Use?

(47:54) What is the Research on Nicotine in the Sporting Realm?

(49:53) Is Nicotine on the Banned Substances List?  

(51:47) 1% Better Challenge


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast


If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Article on Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe 

https://www.cyclingweekly.com/racing/new-team-philosophy-no-foreign-investment-and-red-bull-helmets-inside-the-red-bull-bora-hansgrohe-deal 


Article on All Cause Mortality and Mass Participation Events- 

https://www.researchgate.net/publication/377994179_All-cause_mortality_risks_among_participants_in_mass-participation_sporting_events 


Article About Usain Bolt vs Kylian Mbappe

https://www.goal.com/en/lists/psg-superstar-kylian-mbappe-agrees-100m-sprint-challenge-usain-bolt-rates-chances-against-olympic-legend/blt10ad068f7bf2e17f#cs3081571ff2a39f26 


Articles on Nicotine and Performance- 

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3151730/ 

https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/resources/files/final_report_13c35cc_pr._chiamulera.pdf 

https://www.researchgate.net/publication/346115253_Practitioner_observations_of_oral_nicotine_use_in_elite_sport_You_snus_you_lose

item.title: NIL, Doping & The Kenyan Marathon Team
item.description:
Xylon and David cover some NIL news out of high school, they discuss how absurd the Kenyan Olympic marathon teams are and some strange doping news out of China. They briefly touch on IM Texas results and get to quite a bit of injury news between Bell’s Palsy, a rib stress fracture and some cardiac arrhythmias. They also debut some new branding and May’s 1% Better challenge.


(00:00) Introduction

(01:19) Xylon Doing Some Heat Training

(04:05) Monitoring Heat Adaptation

(06:00) David’s training, Bumping up the Cardiovascular Load and Reducing Mechanical Load 

(07:42) Community Shoutouts 

(07:54) Listener Question about Fueling 

(08:40) Race day Breakfast Recommendations 

(10:05) Gut training and GI distress

(12:55) New Branding for the Podcast

(14:00) Kenyan Marathon Team Selection

(18:19) Eliud Kipchoge’s Chances of Winning Another Olympic Gold in Paris  

(21:10) Addy Ritzenhein’s NIL deal with On running 

(24:18) NIL’s Implications for Competitive Balance 

(27:19) Chinese Doping Story 

(38:00) The Doctor’s Office

(38:17) Joel Embiid’s Bell’s Palsy

(40:05) Lionel Sanders’ Rib Stress Fractures

(42:54) Anna Shackley’s Retirement due to Heart Arrhythmia 

(49:06) IM Texas Brief Recap

(51:52) May’s 1% Better Challenge

item.title: Underfueling in Endurance Athletes
item.description:
After some recapping of the Marathon majors and a potential for an Olympic Duathlon, the guys talk about the prevalence of underfueling in endurance athletes, reviewing a recently released paper. 


(00:00) Introduction 

(00:43) Cycling Feed Zones and Crashes 

(03:59) Xylon’s lack of Training 

(07:16) David’s Training 

(09:00) Community Shoutouts 

(11:32) Why is the Women’s only Marathon World Record so much slower than the Women’s Marathon World Record 

(15:33) Boston Marathon Observations 

(21:03) London Marathon Observations

(24:19) The Seine River Water Quality Issues for the Paris Olympics

(27:08) Underfuellng in Endurance Athletes 

(31:08) Overview of Fuelling in the Study 

(32:10) What were the Carbohydrate Intakes in the Study

(37:27) Caffeine Intake in Endurance Athletes

(39:57) Fluid Intake in Endurance Athletes

(40:25) What are Recommended Fueling Guidelines for Athletes 

(42:10) When to Fuel with Fat & Protein and Flavour Fatigue

(44:53) Recommended Caffeine Intakes and Using Caffeine in a Race

(50:10) Should you Train your Gut for Race Nutrition? 

(53:17) Fueling for Low Intensity vs High Intensity

(59:38) What Causes GI Issues on Race day?

(60:53) Pre Exercise and Post Exercise Nutrition Recommendations

(64:57) 1% Better Challenge


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38257082/

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33371706/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7796021/#:~:text=There%20is%20not%20a%20single,Refuel%2C%20Repair%2C%20and%20Rest.

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4974290/

item.title: Latest in Supershoe Research
item.description:
Episode 7 sees us review Xylon’s Two Oceans run, premier a new segment and briefly review the T1-00 Singapore before diving into the main part of the episode, a timely review of the latest in research on Supershoes. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:25) Xylon’s Two Oceans Review

(08:41) David’s training update 

(11:21) Community Shoutouts 

(12:08) Update on Wout van Aert 

(13:26) New segment announcement The Doctor’s Office 

(13:36) Magnus Ditlev’s Scaphoid Injury 

(16:40) Singapore 100 Jeopardy 

(17:36) Cooling techniques and performing in the heat

(21:00) Update on Supershoe research 

(21:36) How much do supershoes help elite runners?

(29:06) What are the benefits of supershoes to normal runners? 

(41:48) How long do supershoes last?  

(46:15) David’s reason for being a late adopter of supershoes.  

(48:26) Why do supershoes help?

(52:40) How to choose shoes and supershoes

(57:12) How individual are responses to supershoes?


Thanks to Precision Fuel and Hydration for their sponsorship, use the link below to get 15% off your first order and support the podcast: 

https://visit.pfandh.com/theonepercentbetterpodcast

If you’re unsure how which products to use or how to plan your race nutrition check out Precision’s Fuel & Hydration Planner (if you follow this you will have your discount applied automatically on your first order too): 

https://visit.pfandh.com/onepercentfuelplanner


Article on Supershoes Improving Performance of Elites - 

Willwacher S, Mai P, Helwig J, Hipper M, Utku B, Robbin J. Does Advanced Footwear Technology Improve Track and Road Racing Performance? An Explorative Analysis Based on the 100 Best Yearly Performances in the World Between 2010 and 2022. Sports Med Open. 2024 Feb 8;10(1):14. doi: 10.1186/s40798-024-00683-y. PMID: 38332220; PMCID: PMC10853158.

Articles on Supershoes Benefiting Non-elites - 

Paradisis, G. P., Zacharogiannis, E., Bissas, A., & Hanley, B. (2023). Recreational Runners Gain Physiological and Biomechanical Benefits From Super Shoes at Marathon Paces. International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance, 18(12), 1420-1426. Retrieved Mar 25, 2024, from https://doi.org/10.1123/ijspp.2023-0115

Joubert, D. P., Dominy, T. A., & Burns, G. T. (2023). Effects of Highly Cushioned and Resilient Racing Shoes on Running Economy at Slower Running Speeds. International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance, 18(2), 164-170. Retrieved Mar 25, 2024, from https://doi.org/10.1123/ijspp.2022-0227

Article on Running Economy in New & Old EVA vs PEBA shoes - 

Rodrigo-Carranza V, Hoogkamer W, González-Ravé JM, et al. Influence of different midsole foam in advanced footwear technology use on running economy and biomechanics in trained runners. Scand J Med Sci Sports. 2024; 34:e14526. doi:10.1111/sms.14526

Article on Midsole Thickness - 

Zach B. Barrons, John W. Wannop & Darren J. Stefanyshyn (2023) The Influence of Footwear Midsole Thickness on Running Economy and Frontal Plane Ankle Stability, Footwear Science, 15:3, 155-160, DOI: 10.1080/19424280.2023.2218321  

Article on Running Power and Supershoe Selection - 

Joubert, Dustin P.1; Oehlert, Garrett M.2; Jones, Eric J.2; Burns, Geoffrey T.3. Running Power Metrics Inversely Related To Oxygen Consumption In Advanced Racing Shoes: 160. Medicine & Science in Sports & Exercise 55(9S):p 41, September 2023. | DOI: 10.1249/01.mss.0000980096.63719.db

Article on Variability of Running Economy between Individuals in different Supershoes-

Knopp M, Muñiz-Pardos B, Wackerhage H, Schönfelder M, Guppy F, Pitsiladis Y, Ruiz D. Variability in Running Economy of Kenyan World-Class and European Amateur Male Runners with Advanced Footwear Running Technology: Experimental and Meta-analysis Results. Sports Med. 2023 Jun;53(6):1255-1271. doi: 10.1007/s40279-023-01816-1. Epub 2023 Mar 2. PMID: 36862339; PMCID: PMC10185608.

Article on Shoe Comfort and Running Economy -  

Van Alsenoy K, van der Linden ML, Girard O, Santos D. Increased footwear comfort is associated with improved running economy - a systematic review and meta-analysis. Eur J Sport Sci. 2023 Jan;23(1):121-133. doi: 10.1080/17461391.2021.1998642. Epub 2021 Nov 21. PMID: 34726119.

item.title: Geezers, Injuries and Rugby
item.description:
This week’s episode sees us discuss the new world club rugby cup, some research on chest fairings, transitioning from rugby 15s to 7s, the hardest geezer and Team Visma-Lease a Bike’s ongoing crash headaches. 


(00:00) Introduction

(01:14) Dave’s running and time at altitude

(02:45) Dave’s Berlin Half Marathon review

(05:15) Xylon’s pre for Two Oceans ultra marathon 

(08:05) Community Shoutouts 

(09:15) Jonas Vingegaard’s crash, injuries and what it means for the Tour de France 

(16:57) What is cycling doing to help prevent crashes and are we seeing more than normal this year?

(20:45) Research on chest fairings and quantifying their improvement in performance

(25:08) Players transitioning from rugby 15s to rugby 7s 

(29:01) The Hardest Geezer and some challenges he faced 

(33:00) Announcement of the Rugby Club World Cup 

(36:34) Scheduling issues for the Club World Cup and what it means for player safety

item.title: Mud, Drugs and Tears
item.description:
In this episode we touch on some rule changes in Ironman, World cross country championships, Wout van Aert’s big crash and what it means for Visma-Lease a Bike and a HUGE drug scandal in Australian sport.

item.title: The Role of Siblings in Sporting Development, Barkley Marathons & the NFL Combine
item.description:
This week we discuss some rugby news, Wales being called out by Georgia and planning of world cup cycles, running a squad and team cohesion vs best individuals. We also touch on some American sports, discussing the success of Women’s college basketball and why it’s outperforming the men’s, why the NFL combine is the worst job interview ever and we finish off talking about The Barkley Marathons - the race that eats its young. 


(00:00) Introduction

(00:10) Episode Starts

(01:41) Why is David’s heart rate lower in the UK compared to Australia

(04:39) Xylon’s training update and why he is doing back to back runs 

(12:00) Ireland’s performance in the 6 Nations and what’s driving it

(16:51) World cup cycles in rugby, squad management and team cohesion

(23:20) Wales get called out by Georgia and tiers of teams in World Rugby 

(28:00) Louis Rees-Zammit and his NFL aspirations

(32:02) The NFL Combine being outdated 

(39:00) The Women’s domination over the Men in March Madness 

(44:30) Developing athletes and being challenged by people bigger and better 

(48:02) The Barkley Marathons 


Links Mentioned - 

Georgia Challenging Wales -

​​https://www.instagram.com/p/C40O_nIMSlG/?igsh=eGsxNmNvbGJqOThn

item.title: Swapping Gym for Gymnastics & Winning Gold - Nicolai Morris
item.description:
In this week’s episode Xylon and Dave talk to Nicolai Morris about her work with one of the world’s best high jumpers Nicola Olyslagers and their non-conventional approach to getting Nicola ready for her recent world indoor championships gold medal. 


(00:00) Episode Starts

(01:00) David and an explanation of high heart rate during training

(04:38) Xylon and training and back to back long runs

(06:46) Is wrist heart rate reliable?

(08:55) Community shoutouts

(10:29) Camille Herron update

(13:40) Lil Nas X and running NYC half marathon in designer sneakers

(15:26) Interview with Nicolai starts

(17:33) Why are the team’s Nicolai worked with hated?

(19:27) How did Nicolai get into sport?

(21:17) How did Nicolai get into strength and conditioning?

(22:37) What made Nicolai go into teaching?

(24:10) How did Nicolai get into pole vaulting 

(27:28) How do you learn to do the pole vault?

(29:43) What is Nicolai’s highest pole vault?

(31:44) What is talent?

(36:06) How did Nicolai start working with Nicola Olyslagers?

(39:50) Adding gymnastics and removing traditional strength work from Nicola’s program

(47:25) Today’s society, less unstructured play and what this is doing to modern athletes’ movement competencies 

(52:26) If designing the perfect curriculum to create the world’s best athletes, what would Niciolai do with children through early adolescence

(58:24) Summary

item.title: Ep 2 - Women vs men as distance gets longer and why you may be overheating
item.description:
In episode 2, we talk through a big interview Jakob Ingebrigsten recently gave where he talked about doping in track and field. “I think doping is worse now than 10 years ago,” Ingebrigtsen told the Times. We take a look at the factors around women closing the gap on men as race distance gets longer and talk about overheating from the recent T100 Miami triathlon race.


(00:00) Episode starts


(00:59) How David hacked jetlag on his trip to Australia


(03:40) Xylon reports back from his first ever 5-stage cycle race


(12:45) What's happening in sport this week


(13:00) WTCS Abu Dhabi canceled for rain


(13:50) Dissecting Jakob Ingebrigsten Times interview


(24:10) Simona Halep returns in Miami open from doping ban


(34:40) Camille Herron breaking all the records


(39:55) Overheating in T100 in Miami


(42:50) What is heat illness


Show notes:

Article on Jakob

https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/jakob-ingebrigtsen-interview-josh-kerr-gqbfzln9g


Article on Simona Halep 

https://www.skysports.com/tennis/news/12040/13089463/simona-halep-confirms-tennis-return-at-miami-open-following-doping-ban


Articles on Heat Illness

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4596225/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9826288/ 

https://www.uptodate.com/contents/exertional-heat-illness-in-adolescents-and-adults-epidemiology-thermoregulation-risk-factors-and-diagnosis/print

item.title: Aero Helmets & Leg Tape - AI Aerodynamics with Robby Ketchell
item.description:
Episode 1 of 1% Better is all about aerodynamics. We chat to one of the world's experts in aerodynamics, Robby Ketchell, about the Visma-Lease a Bike helmet that broke the internet, learnings for cyclists about aero position and some aerodynamics and their impact on running. We also touch on AI and aerodynamics.

(00:00) Episode starts

(00:56) Xylon and Dave updates

(06:50) What’s come across our desks

(06:55) Tokyo Marathon

(07:53) Eliud Kipchoge and his Legacy

(09:50) Sifan Hassan

(11:15) Who’s the New Face of World Marathoning

(13:20) London Marathon Fields

(15:53) T100 Miami Fields and Withdrawals

(17:22) Oura & Strava Integration

(18:55) Community Shoutouts

(21:30) Interview with Robby Ketchell

(23:45) Is the New Visma-Lease a Bike Helmet Safe?

(26:39) How do Innovations like this Helmet come about?

(30:02) What is the Iteration of a Project like this like?

(33:48) What are the Key Factors in Aerodynamics of Cycling?

(36:17) Why do Gloves Help Aerodynamics?

(37:06) The Marketing Aspects of a Helmet like this

(39:31) Will this Helmet be the Difference in the Tour de France?

(39:52) What Percentage Improvement Could you Expect from this Helmet?

(40:56) Robby’s thoughts on why team UAE have Improved so much this year?

(43:25) Aero chat – AI aerodynamics

(45:20) The Use Case for Aero chat and Zwift doping

(47:16) Kipchoge’s and Shin Tape for Aerodynamics

(48:51) Why wouldn’t Kipchoge use socks instead of tape?

(49:41) How much does Tape like this help Kipchoge and Why?

(51:12) What are the Aerodynamic Benefits of a Bunch when Climbing?

(56:34) Summary


Article on Helmet Regulations:

https://road.cc/content/news/uci-review-helmet-design-rules-307123

Piece Robby Wrote:

https://www.aero.chat/analysis

Robby’s Website Mentioned:
https://Aero.chat 


Follow us on Social:

Instagram

https://www.instagram.com/onepercentbetterpod?igsh=MW1tcGM4MHQ4bm5oaw==

Tik Tok

https://vm.tiktok.com/ZMMYf54KB/


Royalty Free Music from Tunetank.com Track: Trendy Style by PillowVibes https://tunetank.com/track/1027-trendy-style/

item.title: Introduction
item.description:
Join our hosts Xylon van Eyck and David Lipman as they introduce themselves, the podcast and briefly discuss some interesting changes to the track & field landscape with the upcoming 'Drive to Survive-like' Netflix series for track & field, an announcement from Seb Coe that there will be more frequent World Championships and the news of Michael Johnson fronting a new track & field league.


Follow the podcast at: https://www.instagram.com/onepercentbetterpod


Submit topics, feedback or questions to us on Instagram via DM or to the email address: podcast.1percentbetter@gmail.com
